206949.explanation: In major decisions, residents must have real influence early on. A “citizens’ panel” with non-binding advice is not enough. Residents should participate and provide input before choices and budgets are finalized. The council decides and remains responsible. Illusory influence causes frustration.

206950.explanation: The municipality must actively urge the national government on this. One well-staffed municipal team with a single point of contact prevents fragmentation and frustration, provided it truly leads to faster processing and reduced administrative burden. Not an additional layer of government, but efficient, concrete and local.

206951.explanation: Groningen is expensive and poor at the same time. The costs have been too easily passed on to residents, entrepreneurs and associations. The property tax (OZB) can be lowered through discipline and efficiency. The total OZB amount should increase at most in line with inflation; increases in property values should not automatically lead to higher OZB.

206952.explanation: We strive for the most efficient organization possible, with direct contact that fosters trust and quick processing. Students have a crucial role in this and must participate with real influence, not just as a symbolic form of involvement.

206953.explanation: We support circular and sustainable procurement, but with common sense. Local purchasing is often the most sustainable: short supply chains, less transport, more control over quality. This also strengthens the Groningen economy, creates jobs and keeps community funds in the region.

206954.explanation: Heritage and local traditions are valuable, but should not consume the majority of cultural subsidies. Cultural policy must be balanced and also allow space for new, diverse and innovative initiatives so that culture remains lively and future-oriented.

206955.explanation: Youth care (including youth protection) is structurally under pressure and too often fails vulnerable children. Due to increasing shortages (Ravijn year), there must be no cuts here. Cut bureaucracy and fragmentation, not help. Organize youth care close to the child, school and neighborhood.

206956.explanation: The begging ban must remain to limit nuisance and protect livability, but always linked to effective support for vulnerable groups so that help takes precedence over punishment.

206957.explanation: Pressure on healthcare is high and financial shortages (Ravijn year) threaten to further impair accessibility and quality. Funds for climate policy could be redirected to healthcare, focusing on less administration and management and more direct support for people who need care.

206958.explanation: Completely excluding companies that use fossil fuels is unfeasible and symbolic. Realistic and effective is to apply sustainability requirements and incentives in municipal contracts so that sustainability remains concrete, feasible and affordable.

206959.explanation: The terrace tax must be abolished. The revenue is limited and does not outweigh the burdens on horeca entrepreneurs. Abolishing it stimulates a lively city, strengthens the local economy and reduces unnecessary bureaucracy.

206960.explanation: The revenue is limited and the way it was introduced has damaged trust in the municipality. Less bureaucracy and working with stakeholders to prevent so-called “clutter”, not through unnecessary and misplaced advertising tax.

206961.explanation: We are positive about additional investment where backlogs are greatest, but will certainly not neglect vulnerable residents in so-called ‘richer’ neighborhoods for convenience. Policy must be purposeful and fair. Penalizing by postal code helps no one and increases inequality.

206962.explanation: Shopkeepers and horeca determine their own opening hours. No patronizing or harassment by the municipality. Entrepreneurs know what works, adjust supply to demand and thus ensure freedom, a lively city and a strong local economy.

206963.explanation: Emergency shelter is national policy and a legal obligation. That responsibility and structural solutions lie with the national government. We do not provide shelter for safe countries and take our own local challenges into account, because there are plenty.

206964.explanation: We choose a fair distribution of scarce housing. The housing shortage is not caused by specific groups, but by years of insufficient construction and poor flow. The solution lies with the municipality: faster building and better use of the existing housing stock.

206965.explanation: Only people in an ongoing asylum procedure receive temporary, humane shelter. For safe countries, the municipality stops housing to prevent Europe from becoming unnecessarily attractive and to protect its own resources and challenges.

206966.explanation: Employers are responsible for housing labor migrants on or near the workplace. This is practical, prevents extra pressure on neighborhoods and facilities, and promotes livability. The municipality sets requirements and supervises, but bears no responsibility itself.

206967.explanation: No automatic Stadjerspas for all refugees. Support first focuses on basics: housing, language, work and healthcare. Additional provisions can then promote inclusion. The criterion is income/poverty line, not origin or status.

206968.explanation: We encourage sustainable business operations but do not exclude companies. Sustainability and economy must go hand in hand. Realistic requirements and support, no symbolic policy. Not every company can operate fully sustainably, while their products or services are needed.

206969.explanation: Angling remains permitted, but always safe, responsible and under strict enforcement of permits, policies and fishing techniques.

206970.explanation: We do not ban terrace heating, not entrepreneur-friendly. Allow freedom, the city stays lively and sustainability is encouraged where possible. No extra policy on top of existing problems; first get basics, safety and other priorities properly arranged.

206971.explanation: More frequent mowing is not the goal. We choose smart mowing: only where functional, safe and necessary, and less where ecologically valuable. This preserves biodiversity, ensures visibility and accessibility, and avoids useless work.

206972.explanation: Mandatory conversion away from gas is bad; residents retain freedom of choice. Such a measure increases costs and energy poverty. Sustainability can be achieved through advice, subsidies and guidance without unnecessarily burdening residents financially.

206973.explanation: Groningen must sell Warmtestad. The municipality should not play entrepreneur – experience shows it is not good at it. Supervision and control are handled by professionals so that heating remains reliable, affordable and efficient, without extra costs for residents.

206974.explanation: We do not want solar panel fields on farmland or open landscapes. Through smart alternatives on roofs, facades and high-rise buildings, we protect landscape and agriculture, reduce network congestion and prevent extra burdens. Sustainable yes, but not at the expense of the environment or livability.

206975.explanation: New forest should only be planted where it truly makes sense. Use native species and ecologically responsible methods that enhance biodiversity and recreation. Forest does not fit everywhere and should not be an end in itself in open landscapes.

206976.explanation: We allow longer events at multiple locations, provided permits are issued on time and in consultation with directly involved parties and residents. We want spreading and coordination, this ensures a lively offer with minimal nuisance. And supports the local economy and social activities.

206977.explanation: Full sustainability of events is unattainable in the short term. The “most sustainable festival site Stadspark” sometimes resembles more a generator park during large festivals. Events may continue, even if not fully sustainable; sustainability is encouraged where possible.

206978.explanation: Safety has been our top priority for years; violence is increasing, not just against women. A safe space will be created in entertainment areas for victims of sexual violence and intimidation. Enforcement and strict supervision remain crucial: prevention is better than cure.

206979.explanation: The current number of festivals remains. Distribution over multiple locations, including small festivals, prevents overload and keeps culture and economy lively. Variety and small-scale initiatives are valuable for the local economy and social cohesion.

206980.explanation: Multiple testing locations should be established in entertainment areas so visitors can make informed and safe choices and reduce risks of drug use. As always, we focus on health, safety and prevention without limiting nightlife culture.

206981.explanation: We have strived for years to make sports free for youth and want free membership up to age 18! This provides equal opportunities for everyone, keeps sport accessible and contributes to health, social development and well-being.

206982.explanation: Naming new streets and squares after resistance heroes from the slavery past is possible, but not mandatory. Each neighborhood should weigh carefully and proportionately, with attention to other priorities and practical choices.

206983.explanation: We want the annual memorial event to be organized by social organizations themselves and for the municipality to only support and set frameworks. This provides recognition, influence and community spirit without too much municipal interference.

206984.explanation: These local traditions must be preserved, but with attention to safety, environment and responsible use. Traditions, absolutely, but safe and controlled.

206986.explanation: The existing sports and culture pass already meets much of the need. Art and culture programs for children remain important to develop talents and offer equal opportunities regardless of background. Extra funding is not always necessary.

206987.explanation: Supporters of FC Groningen should have a stronger voice and be able to develop co-ownership. The municipality facilitates this but does not remain an owner itself. This keeps the club locally anchored, retains supporters’ influence and limits political interference.

206988.explanation: Just recovered from a possible financial blow around the Nieuwe Poort, a new prestige project threatens to be forced upon us. A pop venue: yes, but affordable, feasible and fitting with existing locations like the Oosterpoort or Martinihal. South side of station: too expensive and too risky.

206989.explanation: Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) may carry a baton, provided they are well trained and use it proportionally. No policy on top of policy, but enforcement receives the necessary priority: more decisiveness to keep the atmosphere in the city center pleasant for everyone.

206990.explanation: Municipal enforcement officers must act visibly neutral. Uniformity is fundamental for authority, recognizability, and trust. Diversity is important, but neutrality takes precedence in enforcement; religious expressions do not fit here.

206991.explanation: The number of enforcement officers (BOA’s) will be doubled, focusing on enforcement and neighborhood-oriented work. This way, we gain insight into local problems, increase preventive supervision, and maintain closer contact with residents. In neighborhoods, we also want to work with neighborhood fathers and mothers alongside BOA's.

206992.explanation: More camera surveillance will be installed in the city center and problem neighborhoods: targeted, proportional, and privacy-conscious. Enforcement will become more effective and safety will increase, without endless policies. The priority is action, not just creating policies.

206993.explanation: Allocation is not the problem; affordability is. Focusing on symbolic measures hides the real issue: too few homes have been built in recent decades. Rules about "local ties" are arbitrary and unclear.

206994.explanation: Automatic priority for specific professions in social housing is not a solution. Many people in these professions are not eligible anyway. The core problem is the housing shortage. Accelerate the construction of affordable rental and owner-occupied homes for everyone.

206995.explanation: Agreed, with clear frameworks. High-rise buildings are necessary in urban areas to use space efficiently, but only if they fit the environment. Clear, simple high-rise policies prevent arbitrariness and excesses. No skyscrapers on postage-stamp plots, but thoughtful construction.

206996.explanation: Rules about owner-occupation do not solve the housing shortage and are mainly symbolic. They can also harm rental and student housing. Limit the extremes, but address the real problem: too few affordable homes. Build more, faster, and affordably.

206997.explanation: Vacancy undermines livability. After six months of vacancy, a tax can incentivize turnover, with exemptions if delays are caused by the municipality. Actively convert offices into housing. For long-term vacancy (2+ years), expropriation can be considered.

206998.explanation: Abolishing aesthetic regulations solves nothing. Quality of the built environment remains necessary. The problem is arbitrariness, not the rules. Reform the system: transparent, professional, and differentiate where recognized designers work. No to abolishing, yes to adjusting!

206999.explanation: Student housing is not a core municipal task but a housing market issue. Under the new rental law, there are actually opportunities for housing corporations and investors. The municipality must allow space and stop blocking. Hanze and RUG share responsibility; focus on broad housing needs.

207000.explanation: New construction on agricultural land is possible, but only after existing plans and inner-city locations have been utilized. Vital Green Lungs remain protected; choices are focused, careful, and not arbitrary. Densification alone is often insufficient.

207002.explanation: Distribution of social rental housing is good but not rigidly enforced. Build first, then distribute logically with attention to support and local situation. Currently, the municipality often selectively focuses on projects in which it has its own interest; livability is paramount.

207003.explanation: People on social assistance who can work can participate if it is meaningful and appropriate. We choose pragmatic customization and guidance, not ideological obligations or coercion; not everyone can or needs to contribute.

207004.explanation: Basic jobs for people distant from the labor market deserve space, but only if they are real, meaningful jobs that offer perspective, job satisfaction, and self-reliance. When used practically, they strengthen employment and social stability. A basic job is serious, not a box-ticking exercise.

207006.explanation: People on social assistance who can work contribute to society. Current policy promotes dependency; we choose a pragmatic approach that encourages participation and values effort.

207007.explanation: Children from low-income families receive extra support so sports, culture, and activities are accessible to everyone. Poverty knows no postal code; equal opportunities and participation come first.

207008.explanation: The fraud reporting point remains to prevent abuse, protect resources, and maintain trust in social provisions. Discontinuing it would undermine honesty and trust.

207009.explanation: Agreed, but with nuance: extra car-free streets can be locally useful for safety or livability, as long as accessibility for residents, entrepreneurs, and the disabled is guaranteed.

207010.explanation: Do not reduce the number of parking spaces further; there are already too few in new construction. Fewer spaces lead to frustration and can endanger traffic safety. Residents, entrepreneurs, and the disabled must have sufficient parking opportunities.

207011.explanation: Lower and keep parking fees affordable; use revenues for mobility in and to villages and neighborhoods. Stop treating parking as a cash cow. First get the municipal budget in order; no more shifting burdens onto residents, entrepreneurs, or associations.

207012.explanation: More bicycle parking spaces are welcome, but this must not automatically come at the expense of car parking spaces. Residents, entrepreneurs, and the disabled must be able to keep the city center accessible. Mobility policy requires customization, not one-sided choices.

207013.explanation: Environmental zones may remain to improve air quality and sustainability, but rules must be realistic and not force small entrepreneurs, like market traders, to buy expensive electric vehicles.

207014.explanation: 30 km/h limits apply only where it makes sense: in residential neighborhoods for safety and livability. Main roads, access roads, and ring roads remain higher for traffic flow. General 30 km/h is unnecessary, hard to enforce, and can cause insecurity.

207015.explanation: We invest in voluntary, well-guided exit programs for sex workers so they can safely and sustainably choose another work or life direction. The focus is on health, protection, and social participation, without coercion.

207016.explanation: Mandatory food gardens are undesirable. Residents can start initiatives themselves via Right to Challenge, tailored to local needs and under their own neighborhood management. This keeps initiative with residents, stimulates involvement, and uses resources efficiently.

206949.explanation: A citizens' assembly increases involvement, quality, and support in decision-making and allows youth to better participate than regular participation. Due to high costs, we want to use this only on major, important issues that require broad support.

206950.explanation: The government has neglected Groningen for years and still falls short. Victims must be central with fast, generous compensation and clarity. One municipal team can reduce bureaucracy, increase speed, and provide more clarity.

206951.explanation: Property tax applies only to homeowners. We find it fair that they pay a bit more tax than renters, who already have much higher average monthly costs. Also, reducing property tax now will further worsen the position of young people in the housing market...

206952.explanation: The municipality insufficiently reaches students and too often shifts student issues to educational institutions, while it itself is responsible for housing, welfare, or sports. A student affairs department run partly by students can better address problems that currently remain unresolved.

206953.explanation: Student & City stands for a sustainable, green, and healthy Groningen! By purchasing as circularly and sustainably as possible as a municipality, we set a good example, stimulate innovation, and build the economy of the future.

206954.explanation: Student & City wants more money for culture in general! More for events, nightlife culture, incubators, but also local traditions such as Groningen’s Relief Day. Local traditions can be supported without compromising new cultural initiatives or festivals.

206955.explanation: Student & City believes youth care is no place to cut budgets. Waiting times are long and problems are significant. Cutting now leads to higher societal costs later, such as school dropout or crime. Investing in youth is investing in the future.

206956.explanation: No one may be punished for homelessness or rooflessness. Passive begging must be possible. However, a ban on aggressive begging is necessary so police can intervene when situations get out of control.

206957.explanation: Care and climate are not opposites. Stopping climate policy damages our future: heat and water nuisance are increasing and already pose health risks. We must reduce CO₂ emissions and make the City greener and cooler!

206958.explanation: We must work towards a future-proof and energy-neutral Groningen. This will not happen overnight but requires the structural phasing out of fossil fuels in the long term. This goal also applies to the municipality itself and companies it deals with.

206959.explanation: Terraces make Groningen lively and attractive. By relaxing rules and lowering terrace taxes, we give entrepreneurs more space, create a nicer streetscape, and stimulate the local economy!

206960.explanation: We are not fans of an advertising tax: it yields almost nothing due to expensive implementation, but places extra costs on entrepreneurs. At the same time, the tax is new and the evaluation still has to take place. Abolishing before that evaluation is far too premature.

206961.explanation: It is unacceptable that your neighborhood or street determines how many opportunities you get. Differences are large even in Groningen. By investing unequally in equal opportunities, we strengthen livability and prospects for residents of vulnerable neighborhoods.

206962.explanation: Student & City advocates for a vibrant City, both day and night! We must protect free closing times for nightlife and allow local bakers to sell their bread before 12 noon on Sundays.

206963.explanation: Student & City stands for humane and constitutional refugee reception. If every municipality takes responsibility, we can address the reception and flow crisis and relieve Ter Apel.

206964.explanation: Municipalities are obliged to accommodate status holders because of their legitimate reason to be here, such as war. Priority is needed for equal treatment due to their unfair position (Constitution Article 1), and without priority they end up homeless or in expensive emergency shelters, which is worse and more costly for everyone.

206965.explanation: Basic provisions (such as a bed, bath, bread) are necessary for rejected asylum seekers who cannot leave yet, for example if their papers for return are not in order. Without shelter they are on the street. This is inhumane and causes higher social costs.

206966.explanation: Employers actively bring in labor migrants and have a responsibility to house them, preferably close to the workplace. When this falls short, it must be solved together with the municipality.

206967.explanation: By making culture, sports and recreation accessible, we improve the integration of newcomers. Actually, Student & Stad wants a pass for everyone. Following Rotterdam’s example, we thus stimulate the local economy while making culture, sports and recreation accessible for vulnerable residents!

206968.explanation: Student & Stad wants to build a sustainable and energy-neutral city, but a stop on establishments is unrealistic. Companies must also be given the chance to become more sustainable. Other measures are more effective than this ban.

206969.explanation: Student & Stad thinks it is important to take animals into account, but a ban on rod fishing is simply impossible. People will keep fishing and enforcement is not feasible. Also, rod fishing is already regulated and that is sufficient for now.

206970.explanation: Student & Stad understands the sustainability objections against terrace heating, but a ban mainly punishes visitors and local entrepreneurs. We do think the municipality should issue subsidies and cheap loans to encourage entrepreneurs to move to more sustainable solutions.

206971.explanation: Our biodiversity is under pressure. By mowing less often we relieve this pressure while saving on implementation costs. However, the municipality must ensure that less mowing does not endanger the safety of rowers or cyclists at intersections.

206972.explanation: In the long term, neighborhoods must get off gas and sometimes mandates are needed to take that step together. It is essential, however, that residents are not saddled with high costs and that the municipality provides good guidance and affordable alternatives.

206973.explanation: Warmtestad accelerates the local energy transition like crazy! We oppose full privatization and also full municipal ownership. A mixed form maintains control over affordability and quality without the municipality bearing all financial risks.

206974.explanation: Student & Stad sees much added value here. We need lots of sustainable energy and space is scarce. We do not oblige farmers to anything but give them room to take faster steps together in the energy transition!

206975.explanation: More forest is good for the climate, biodiversity and health! This is a good goal to work towards in the long term. We also want more green parks and places where people can meet and relax.

206976.explanation: Events make Groningen lively and attractive. Many events must already stop at 11 p.m. By allowing them to go on longer in the evening, we give more room to culture, economy and a vibrant city!

206977.explanation: Sustainability is important, but allowing only sustainable events is unrealistic. We prefer to help organizers become more sustainable than to let events disappear due to overly strict rules.

206978.explanation: Everyone must feel safe and taken seriously when going out. With safe spaces, the municipality shows that it cares about well-being and that people have a place to go if they feel unsafe or unwell.

206979.explanation: Definitely not! Festivals and events make Groningen fun and vibrant. The problem is not the number of festivals, but the lack of suitable locations. Student & Stad actually wants more festival locations in the city!

206980.explanation: Drug testing prevents dangerous situations but is poorly accessible now. Politically you can look away from this, but we know people use drugs. It is safer if people can test and know what they take.

206981.explanation: Every child must be able to do sports. Free sports membership for children under 16 is a good goal. In implementing this, we first give priority to families who cannot afford the membership fees themselves.

206982.explanation: Groningen played an active role in the history of slavery. By naming streets and squares after resistance heroes, we acknowledge this past and make room for a fairer and more inclusive story of our city.

206983.explanation: The history of slavery deserves ongoing attention. By commemorating together with social organizations, we ensure recognition, education and connection among residents.

206984.explanation: Local traditions belong to Groningen! With clear agreements about safety, environment and nuisance, activities such as carbidschieten and Easter fires can continue without harming the surroundings.

206986.explanation: Culture, sports and recreation must be accessible to everyone. Culture is important for the development of children and youth. By bringing them into contact with it, we stimulate creativity and talent.

206987.explanation: In Germany this is the norm and we see positive effects. However, it leads to half as many investors. For FC Groningen this would therefore only be wise if it became standard for all clubs in the Eredivisie.

206988.explanation: Groningen deserves a large music center with a big pop hall. The south side of the station is a good location. If that turns out too expensive, the Suikerterrein is a serious and good alternative.

206989.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) increasingly take over police tasks and often find themselves in unsafe situations. Therefore, we are in favor of a pilot with batons, provided BOAs are trained for this and the baton may only be used for self-defense.

206990.explanation: Enforcers must be a reflection of our society. By allowing room for religious and cultural expressions, more people feel represented and we give BOAs the freedom to be themselves.

206991.explanation: If necessary, we are of course open to more BOA capacity for a safer city, but doubling it is absolutely impossible. It is unnecessary and unfeasible. It also does not solve structural safety problems.

206992.explanation: In the entertainment district, cameras can greatly aid detection or quicker intervention in disturbances to prevent escalation. Outside the entertainment area and known spots, however, we are cautious because cameras mainly shift problems and infringe privacy.

206993.explanation: Reserving half of rental homes is too much and excludes groups such as students or newcomers. Connection with Groningen must not become such a decisive criterion in a tight housing market.

206994.explanation: Teachers, healthcare workers and police are essential for our city. By giving them priority for social rental homes, we ensure that they can continue to live and work here and that Groningen keeps functioning!

206995.explanation: The housing shortage is large. By building higher we can house more people. We always take livability, the streetscape and the environment into account.

206996.explanation: Student & Stad believes a house is for living, but this measure is unfeasible. This would mean the rental sector disappears, to the detriment of, for example, students. A more effective measure against vacancy or speculation is a vacancy tax.

206997.explanation: Definitely! There is already too little space in Groningen. Student & Stad wants speculators to no longer deliberately leave properties vacant while there is a housing shortage. Of course, this only applies to long-term vacancy and we consider the reason for the vacancy.

206998.explanation: Groningen is a beautiful city and that is partly thanks to our aesthetic rules. Student & Stad values these rules but finds them currently a bit too strict. Abolishing them is foolish, but relaxing them seems like a good idea so that housing construction or dealing with earthquake damage can proceed faster!

206999.explanation: Students belong to Groningen. Many student houses are now being converted into studios or apartments. The municipality must invest much more money in affordable student housing so that students can also live, study and contribute to the city comfortably!

207000.explanation: Many more homes are needed in Groningen! We prefer to build in the city but are also open to housing construction in villages if farmers make their land available.

207002.explanation: It is important for Groningen that people with different incomes and backgrounds live in the same neighborhood. This creates more social cohesion and mutual tolerance. Therefore, spreading social housing is important!

207003.explanation: Doing socially valuable work for benefits can be just in our view. Someone must be able to do this, however, and it must never come at the expense of their physical or mental health.

207004.explanation: Basic jobs help people with a distance to the labor market to participate. They increase self-confidence, involvement and provide structure, which leads to more people actively participating in society.

207006.explanation: Forcing people into work that does not suit them backfires. More sustainable solutions arise through guidance towards suitable work, so that people do not fall back into welfare.

207007.explanation: Children are the future. A bicycle, laptop, and school expenses are very important for children's development. If families cannot afford these, the municipality must step in.

207008.explanation: We must combat fraud. Having the option to report it is important for that purpose. Of course, we check whether the report is justified before taking action.

207009.explanation: In the past, too much space was given to cars. Now we prefer to encourage walking, cycling, or public transport. Streets where cars are 'guests' create a more pleasant living environment and make the City healthier, safer, and more sustainable.

207010.explanation: There are far too few bicycle parking spaces. There need to be many more! It must also remain possible to park a car near home or in front of the door when moving, despite the growing population. Due to limited space, we will prioritize bicycle parking spaces.

207011.explanation: This is not smart and unnecessary! Lower parking fees increase parking pressure, making it harder for residents to find a spot. Groningen's rates are cheaper than other major cities, and we prefer to encourage walking, cycling, and public transport. This is safer, healthier, and more sustainable!

207012.explanation: Groningen is a cycling city, but it is becoming increasingly difficult to park your bike at your doorstep or it is being towed away again! This must be fixed, and where necessary, one car parking spot may be replaced by 12 bicycle parking spaces according to us!

207013.explanation: Bad idea! Our environmental zones work well and make Groningen more sustainable and healthier. Introducing these zones was an important step to prepare the City for the future and reduce emissions and nuisance.

207014.explanation: In the past, too much space was given to cars. Now we prefer to encourage walking, cycling, or public transport. This is healthier, safer, and more sustainable. Making streets 30 km/h helps with this and improves the living climate of residential neighborhoods.

207015.explanation: When sex workers want to stop, they sometimes need help; the municipality must always be able to offer that help so that no one is forced into sex work unwillingly.

207016.explanation: Besides that this simply isn't possible in some neighborhoods, such as the city center, we believe the municipality should not impose this on residents. However, the municipality must actively cooperate if neighborhood residents want to set up a food garden!

206949.explanation: Participation is very important to our party. A citizens' assembly increases involvement and trust in the municipality. By lot, people who are normally not heard also come to the table, leading to better considered and more broadly supported decisions. This is how we work together on Groningen.

206950.explanation: Affected individuals should not be sent from pillar to post; that is unacceptable. One team can provide overview, speed, and clarity. It is the government's responsibility to prevent residents from getting stuck in bureaucracy and paperwork.

206951.explanation: Housing costs are already high, and buying a house is difficult enough. Lowering property tax gives residents and entrepreneurs financial room and prevents living and working from becoming unnecessarily expensive. As an alternative, the property tax could also be frozen.

206952.explanation: Students have valuable experience and insights, but policy must serve the interests of all residents. More involvement of students can help, but only if it is purposeful and does not lead to extra bureaucracy or disproportionate attention to one group.

206953.explanation: The municipality, as a major purchaser, has influence. By purchasing sustainably, it stimulates innovation, reduces environmental pressure, and sets a good example. The municipality must remain realistic when procuring: sometimes no alternatives are yet available.

206954.explanation: Heritage and traditions, such as Noorderzon, Gronings Ontzet, and the Easter fires, bring people together and show who we are as a city and region. They make Groningen unique and therefore deserve lasting support from the municipality.

206955.explanation: The government should not only look at its own bank account but also at the future of children and families. Further cuts in youth care cause bigger problems later. Good and timely help prevents heavier care and higher costs.

206956.explanation: The begging ban helps prevent aggressive, intrusive, and annoying begging. The ban prevents nuisance and unsafe situations in public spaces, while assistance can be better organized through other channels. The ban is aimed at begging, not the beggars themselves.

206957.explanation: Care and climate are both very important. Climate problems actually cause extra health and care costs, so investing in climate policy remains necessary. It is tempting to look only at the budget, but as a municipality, you must also look ahead.

206958.explanation: A complete ban is idealistic but not realistic. The municipality must steer towards sustainability and transition without making itself fully dependent on a limited supply.

206959.explanation: Without an attractive environment, the city center will die out. Terraces contribute to vibrancy and the local economy. Lower charges help hospitality entrepreneurs and make the city center more attractive.

206960.explanation: Advertising tax mainly affects local entrepreneurs, who pay for their own window or sign. Abolishing it results in fewer rules and a lively shopping street. Large companies (multinationals) hardly feel this tax; small entrepreneurs do.

206961.explanation: Absolute equality is a core point of our party. That requires targeted investments. Extra effort in vulnerable neighborhoods improves safety, health, and cohesion throughout the municipality.

206962.explanation: Entrepreneurs know best when customers come and what they can handle. More freedom helps them do business, makes things livelier and more flexible, without the municipality having to set rules everywhere. For some places, such as villages, exceptions may apply.

206963.explanation: Emergency shelter is sometimes necessary to guarantee humane shelter. The municipality may (and wants to) not leave people out in the cold when other solutions are lacking.

206964.explanation: Both status holders and other house seekers have urgent interests. A generic priority rule does not do justice to scarcity and calls for tailor-made solutions.

206965.explanation: Stopping shelter leads to distressing situations and nuisance. Basic shelter is needed to safeguard dignity and public order. Furthermore, it is unacceptable towards our neighboring municipalities, such as Ter Apel, that we do not support them.

206966.explanation: Employers involved in labor migration are responsible for decent housing. This prevents exploitation, reduces nuisance, and limits extra pressure on residential areas. Currently, we see too often that labor migrants are housed in cramped spaces under poor living conditions.

206967.explanation: The Stadjerspas helps refugees participate in society. Access to sports, culture, and facilities promotes integration, increases self-reliance, and reduces the risk of isolation and dependence on long-term support. Everyone benefits from this.

206968.explanation: A strict exclusion of companies wanting to establish themselves in Groningen is very idealistic but certainly not realistic. The municipality must stimulate sustainability where possible but without hindering our economic development.

206969.explanation: Angling is a permitted and regulated recreational activity. A ban on recreational fishing is disproportionate and unnecessary with proper supervision and enforcement.

206970.explanation: A total ban unnecessarily restricts entrepreneurs. Targeted rules and promotion of efficient alternatives are much more effective than a general ban.

206971.explanation: Mowing less promotes biodiversity and nature value. Excessive mowing is costly and harmful to insects and plants. This is a small choice with many benefits.

206972.explanation: Sustainability must be achievable and affordable. Coercion without good alternatives increases inequality and frustration among residents.

206973.explanation: If the municipality manages the heat supply, affordability, reliability, and democratic control are guaranteed. Heat is a basic provision. We trust public control more than commercial organizations.

206974.explanation: Agricultural land is scarce and important for food production. Solar panels primarily belong on roofs, industrial sites, and infrastructure, not on green grass. The exception is so-called 'solar fields' where local residents also benefit.

206975.explanation: We agree that more forest is important. Groningen is densely built-up, so smart choices for new green areas are needed. It's not just about the number of hectares but the impact: more nature and green for both people and the environment in our municipality.

206976.explanation: Events make Groningen lively and enjoyable. With clear agreements about sound and safety, they can continue longer into the evening without nuisance. The municipality must ensure that the rules are followed.

206977.explanation: Sustainability is important, but a strict rule excludes too many activities. For example, the fair, Gronings Ontzet, Easter fires, and New Year's Eve might no longer be celebrated. It is better to encourage and assist alternatives rather than forbid everything.

206978.explanation: The nightlife in Groningen is no longer what it used to be. We must make strong choices here. A safe space makes it easier to ask for help, increases safety, and helps people behave responsibly during nightlife.

206979.explanation: Festivals are important for the culture and economy of Groningen. They attract visitors, generate income for local businesses, and offer residents the opportunity to come together and enjoy music and art.

206980.explanation: Drug testing makes drug use safer and helps prevent accidents. This way, problems can be detected sooner and lives can be saved. It is a practical way to protect people in nightlife areas.

206981.explanation: Sports are very important for children and young people. They help them stay healthy, grow, and make friends. No child should be excluded because it is too expensive. Everyone should be able to participate.

206982.explanation: Naming streets and squares after resistance heroes from the slavery past acknowledges their courage and contribution. It strengthens awareness of our history, promotes inclusion, and sends a clear signal that Groningen is committed to justice and equality.

206983.explanation: Collaboration ensures an inclusive and supported commemoration that does justice to different perspectives and stories.

206984.explanation: These traditions belong to the local culture of Groningen. With clear safety rules, they can responsibly continue to exist.

206986.explanation: It is important for children and young people to be introduced to art and culture early on. This helps them develop creativity, discover talents, and learn more about the world around them. Art and culture support their broad development.

206987.explanation: It is always good that supporters have more say about the club. But the financial risks, such as bankruptcy, must be carefully considered to prevent FC Groningen and the municipality from getting into financial trouble.

206988.explanation: A large pop venue is very valuable for music and culture in Groningen, but the location and costs must be carefully considered. We keep options open: the venue is welcome, but it must fit with the surroundings and existing facilities. There may be better locations elsewhere in Groningen.

206989.explanation: Arming increases the chance of escalation. Enforcement officers (BOAs) have a law enforcement and de-escalation role; heavier means belong to the police, not BOAs.

206990.explanation: Enforcers represent the government and must act recognizably and neutrally. External neutrality increases authority and is appropriate for government officials.

206991.explanation: More BOAs means greater visibility, faster intervention, and better enforcement, which contributes to safety and livability. With more BOAs there is also more time to enforce on nuisance, violations by fatbikes, and bicycle theft.

206992.explanation: Camera surveillance helps prevent and solve crimes and gives people a safer feeling. In Groningen, this is necessary because the city center has become less safe in recent years. Especially in the nightlife area, additional cameras are important.

206993.explanation: Community ties can be important, but a fixed rule of 50% does not work well with housing shortages and various housing needs. We opt for customization: for each new construction project we look at local needs and social mix, not at one general norm.

206994.explanation: It is not fair to give priority only to teachers, nurses, and police officers. Other housing seekers with urgent problems then remain out of sight. Moreover, there are many other important professions that could also use support.

206995.explanation: High-rise buildings make smart use of limited space and can help address the housing crisis in Groningen. It is important that new apartments fit the charm and architecture of the city, so Groningen maintains its character.

206996.explanation: Those who buy a house must live there themselves. This prevents houses from remaining empty or being resold for profit. This ensures that more homes remain available for people who genuinely want to build a life in Groningen and prevents exploitation of tenants.

206997.explanation: Vacancy is undesirable in times of housing shortage, let alone a housing crisis. A vacancy tax stimulates use and discourages the gradual stagnation of Groningen.

206998.explanation: The aesthetics rules protect the quality and charm of our streets, villages, and neighborhoods. Abolishing them leads to disorder and loss of quality. We want to prevent Groningen from feeling like Belgium.

206999.explanation: Student housing is important, but funds must be fairly distributed across all housing needs. Alternatives such as cooperative living can also contribute, so that both students and other residents get good housing.

207000.explanation: To solve the housing crisis, new construction is sometimes necessary, also on agricultural land. This must be done carefully, so the landscape is preserved and agriculture can continue as much as possible.

207002.explanation: Distribution prevents concentration of poverty and strengthens livability and social cohesion in all neighborhoods.

207003.explanation: Social work can provide people with structure, self-confidence, and participation in society. It is important that the work fits the person, is as voluntary as possible, and does not get in anyone’s way.

207004.explanation: Basic jobs offer security, work experience, and social contribution for people who would otherwise remain outside the labor market. We must not abandon them.

207006.explanation: Not every job fits a person or is feasible. Giving people less money without considering their situation can lead to poverty and does not really solve problems. It is better to offer tailor-made solutions.

207007.explanation: For children, bicycles, laptops, and school costs are very important for their education and development. Every child should have the same chance to participate, regardless of how much money their parents have.

207008.explanation: Fraud prevention protects support for the social system, provided reports are handled carefully and responsibly.

207009.explanation: This can contribute to safety and livability but requires careful consideration of accessibility and support per street.

207010.explanation: Fewer parking spaces without good alternatives makes it difficult for residents and entrepreneurs. Elderly people and others who need a car must be able to park. Car use should not be unnecessarily discouraged.

207011.explanation: Affordable parking ensures the city remains accessible to residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs. Costs should be lower, people-oriented, and practical, so parking does not become a source of income for the municipality but truly serves the people.

207012.explanation: More bike spots are needed, but completely eliminating car parking spaces requires tailor-made approaches and good alternatives.

207013.explanation: The environmental zones in Groningen improve air quality and our health. Abolishing these zones will only worsen air quality and our health.

207014.explanation: A general 30 km/h rule is not necessary everywhere. Variation per road ensures both safety and traffic flow. We want to prevent situations where, for example, fatbikes have to overtake cars on busy roads, as happens in Amsterdam.

207015.explanation: Everyone who wants to leave sex work must have the opportunity to do so. Extra support increases safety, freedom of choice, and perspective for people who want to stop sex work.

207016.explanation: A food garden is valuable but not always feasible or desirable everywhere. The municipality can support these initiatives but should not impose them from above. It is important that such initiatives are started by residents themselves.

206949.explanation: BBB does not consider citizen assemblies a good instrument and believes the municipality must adhere to the established participation regulations. BBB has experienced that the administrative agenda can be imposed on citizens and that the independence of citizen advice is therefore at risk.

206950.explanation: BBB will certainly support improvements in services to municipal residents. BBB believes the municipality should work efficiently on every topic. From that perspective alone, we support a one-stop-shop approach.

206951.explanation: BBB supports a reduction in property tax (OZB) because this increases the purchasing power of residents, reduces burdens for entrepreneurs, and can contribute to lower social rents. This boost to the local economy pays for itself. Municipal efficiency gains compensate for the lower tax revenues.

206952.explanation: BBB is not in favor of new departments. A fresh perspective from a younger generation works wonders in a civil service organization. BBB believes students can be well deployed within existing departments that deal with or affect student affairs and/or student interests.

206953.explanation: BBB advocates short supply chains. Local procurement is part of that. Short supply chains and local purchasing are good expressions of circularity and sustainability. Local entrepreneurs also strengthen the local economy and contribute to employment and social structure.

206954.explanation: BBB considers it very important to preserve local traditions and heritage as a key cornerstone of our Groningen society. Therefore, BBB believes that the majority of subsidies should be allocated to this.

206955.explanation: BBB believes that the municipality must fulfill its legal duty of care to the youth and keep care accessible. Budget cuts must not make help unattainable. Instead of cutting youth care, the municipality should perform its tasks more efficiently and at lower costs.

206956.explanation: BBB thinks the current begging ban falls short because it primarily focuses on annoying or aggressive begging. BBB wants a begging ban that also extends to city and village centers, shopping areas, and tourist locations. Professional beggars must be excluded.

206957.explanation: BBB believes the municipality must comply with its legal duties. If a choice must be made between funding for climate or for care, the preference, given the known problems and urgency, is for funding care (WMO) and youth care.

206958.explanation: BBB supports eco-realism. Municipal services are directly or indirectly generated by fossil fuels. BBB believes municipal politics must not jeopardize the execution of its legal duties and should stop trying to save the world.

206959.explanation: BBB believes that terrace tax may be abolished. We believe that an affordable Groningen nightlife, both in the city and surrounding rural areas, makes an important contribution to the attractiveness of our municipality and adds to employment.

206960.explanation: BBB thinks advertising tax does not solve cluttering and should be abolished as soon as possible. This tax is poorly justified and does not benefit entrepreneurs. Entrepreneurs in the surrounding areas suffer more because the tax revenue is spent in the city.

206961.explanation: BBB believes the municipality must perform its legal duties for all its residents and entrepreneurs regardless of socioeconomic status. This focus on neighborhoods as referred to in the statement shows that this city administration pays more attention and allocates more money to 'the city' than to the surrounding countryside.

206962.explanation: BBB believes that entrepreneurs should have control over successful entrepreneurship. We believe that the store opening hours regulations and establishment framework should be flexible and business-friendly with attention to what the market/consumer wants.

206963.explanation: BBB wants to get rid of expensive emergency shelters. Limiting the influx of asylum seekers by the national government should contribute to this. Only in the event of unforeseen new conflicts on European soil will we make an exception. A request for help from neighboring municipality Westerwolde will be answered.

206964.explanation: We believe status holders occupy the same position as starters and therefore do NOT form a separate category.

206965.explanation: BBB does not want local municipal variations of national policy. The government has abolished the bed-bath-bread arrangement as of January 1, 2025. The municipality of Groningen has funded the scheme for 2025 from public funds and must stop doing so. Efforts must again be made towards return.

206966.explanation: BBB deploys the 'Good Landlord Act' to properly manage housing for migrant workers and knowledge workers. Employers and employment agencies provide good housing for migrant and knowledge workers when they cannot provide this themselves. Preferably on or near the workplace.

206967.explanation: BBB believes the current 'Stadjerspas regulation' sufficiently safeguards the provision of public funds and makes it verifiable for the municipal council. BBB is of the opinion that refugees, if they meet the criteria, may use the scheme.

206968.explanation: BBB wants to eliminate theoretical, ideological barriers that exclude entrepreneurs. We prefer to stimulate sustainability through innovation and cooperation rather than rejecting companies at the gate. This keeps our municipality attractive to entrepreneurs, preserves employment, or even expands it.

206969.explanation: BBB is against a ban on angling. Angling is a low-threshold recreational activity for many residents, providing relaxation, being outdoors, and social connections. Angling is already subject to clear rules.

206970.explanation: BBB opposes a ban on terrace heating. Comfortable terraces are important both for customers and for the earning capacity of our entrepreneurs.

206971.explanation: Biodiversity is important, but safety and livability come first. Shoulders and grass must be mowed where necessary for (traffic) safety and to prevent clutter. Additionally, BBB wants an active mowing policy to combat harmful invasive plants such as ragwort.

206972.explanation: BBB wants everyone to participate in the energy transition, including people with smaller budgets. Mandatory disconnection from gas drives residents to high costs. We leave no one out in the cold and want an affordable and well-functioning alternative for everyone. Coercion is always unacceptable.

206973.explanation: Selling now is unwise. We do not want to hand over our residents to a commercial party that raises user prices. BBB prefers to incorporate WarmteStad and the already invested community money into a smart, affordable, and realistic energy mix.

206974.explanation: BBB does not want solar parks on agricultural land. That land is urgently needed for our food supply. Generating solar energy should primarily take place on roofs of houses, businesses, barns, and coverings of parking lots.

206975.explanation: BBB opposes mandatory hectare targets but supports the greenery ambitions of the province in the Forest and Wood Implementation Program. The planting of new forests must be voluntary, as described in the Implementation Program.

206976.explanation: Events are important for the liveliness, culture, and local economy of our municipality. BBB supports a general extension of the hours during which events may take place to create more space for celebration, meeting, and tradition. This is also important for our youth.

206977.explanation: BBB opposes mandatory sustainability requirements. This kind of policy can particularly affect small and volunteer-driven events. Organizing events should remain feasible, affordable, and above all fun for everyone.

206978.explanation: A 'safe space' creates a false sense of security. BBB wants a truly safe nightlife area and therefore supports the recommendations from the research by Breuer & Intraval. Sexual violence and transgressive behavior are unacceptable, and victims must always be helped quickly and safely.

206979.explanation: BBB sees festivals as an impulse for the local economy and a connecting link between city and countryside. There is no administrative reason to generally limit the number of festivals. The municipality must make room for initiatives that contribute to liveliness and meeting.

206980.explanation: Safe drugs do not exist. BBB is against drug use in public spaces and therefore also against drug testing in nightlife areas. Individuals are expected to be aware of the law regarding drugs. BBB supports the advice of Breuer & Intraval for a safe nightlife.

206981.explanation: BBB believes funds such as B-slim and the Youth Fund Sports & Culture offer parents sufficient opportunities. The municipality must support sports clubs by lowering municipal charges. This results in lower fees, which directly helps youth who play sports.

206982.explanation: BBB believes residents should recognize themselves in the naming of their living environment. We oppose 'must' and favor involving residents in naming streets, squares, or neighborhoods. Politically motivated naming under duress has no societal benefit or support.

206983.explanation: BBB is of the opinion that the national commemoration, which is organized annually in our capital, demands and generates more than sufficient attention for this specific subject from our collective past.

206984.explanation: Easter fires and carbide shooting are traditions that bring our Groningen communities together. BBB therefore finds it important that these traditions are maintained and facilitated by the municipality.

206986.explanation: BBB finds the current programs sufficient. It is also up to parents and schools to include cultural education in upbringing. BBB wants to involve the Groningen language, traditions, and appreciation of our cultural landscape in this.

206987.explanation: BBB believes that the municipality should exit the current ownership structure and sell its share to give FC full space to further develop. We believe the FC belongs to all of us, including the 8,000 supporters waiting for a season ticket.

206988.explanation: BBB feels that a new pop venue will cost the community a lot of money. BBB has often seen, with projects like this, that the municipality greatly exceeded its budget. BBB prefers to invest in renovating De Oosterpoort.

206989.explanation: BBB focuses on better police availability rather than arming BOAs (special enforcement officers). Carrying a baton requires training in use of force. Certifying BOAs for tasks that may involve violence is expensive, cumbersome, and even has waiting lists.

206990.explanation: BBB believes that uniform hats fit us all. 'Uniform' means that the civil servant shows through clothing that they visibly serve the public cause and not their own culture, religion, or beliefs. That is also the purpose for which civil servants are paid.

206991.explanation: BBB does not believe in doubling the BOA formation. BBB prefers to double police efforts in enforcement and investigations. BBB intends to do this by putting pressure on the mayor and thereby prompting the Public Prosecution Service to make prosecution a priority.

206992.explanation: BBB believes there is sufficient camera surveillance. More cameras harm the privacy of residents and well-meaning visitors to the city. BBB supports a tough approach to nuisance and crime: we want perpetrators and troublemakers prosecuted, not just filmed.

206993.explanation: BBB includes this point in its program. We believe the housing ordinance must be adjusted accordingly. The instrument 'Groningen rents' must also be adjusted so that young and old can continue to live in their villages.

206994.explanation: BBB believes that structural increased construction is necessary. Where critical professions require priority for housing, we believe this priority should be realized.

206995.explanation: BBB is open to urban high-rise buildings as part of housing assignments and efficient use of space.

206996.explanation: BBB wants to abolish the home-ownership obligation. It is a municipal restriction that currently perpetuates the housing shortage.

206997.explanation: BBB opposes a vacancy tax and favors engaging in dialogue with property owners. There is already a vacancy law that gives the municipality sufficient leeway.

206998.explanation: BBB supports abolishing the aesthetic committee. Building without aesthetic regulations does not lead to chaos.

206999.explanation: BBB believes the plans for Zernike campus are sufficient and that the city and surrounding area should be opened to the market. The municipality should again issue permits for traditional student housing. Students belong in the heart of society.

207000.explanation: BBB supports inner-city solutions and incremental expansion.

207002.explanation: BBB favors the distribution of social rental housing throughout the municipality, with attention to livability and customized solutions where needed.

207003.explanation: BBB believes no one enjoys being on welfare and that paid work contributes more to society. Therefore, BBB wants to activate people for work, with unpaid activities serving as a useful step towards the labor market.

207004.explanation: BBB considers basic jobs a good means to activate people and get them out of welfare, as demonstrated by the preceding three-year experiment. The municipality should focus on eventual transition of these individuals to the regular labor market.

207006.explanation: BBB believes the work obligation also includes retaining work. Benefit reductions are only allowed when cooperation obligations are violated. Additionally, the municipality must strictly enforce the language requirement: welfare recipients must be able to speak, read, and write Dutch at a proper level.

207007.explanation: BBB believes the current special social assistance offers sufficient scope to support residents. This assistance must be explicitly used as a tool to truly help people forward, with the structural goal of permanently lifting residents out of poverty.

207008.explanation: BBB is the party of community spirit and caring for each other. We oppose complaint centers that pit residents against each other, which can provoke or worsen neighbor disputes. The municipality already has sufficient instruments to effectively perform its statutory oversight tasks.

207009.explanation: BBB opposes pushing out the car. Many residents of our villages and city depend on their ‘sacred cow’. Expanding ‘car welcome’ streets does not improve accessibility and thus livability. It can also hinder entrepreneurs.

207010.explanation: BBB opposes reducing parking spaces and wants to revert to the parking policy before January 1, 2025. The current policy, combined with removing spots, helps no one. It increases social inequality, disguises an environmental measure, and affects elderly and care-dependent residents.

207011.explanation: BBB supports low parking fees and adjusting the current zoning applied. Parking is not a luxury product. High fees combined with too few parking spaces make the city less accessible. Affordable parking contributes to a livable and accessible city for everyone.

207012.explanation: BBB advocates sufficient bicycle parking spaces in the (inner) city but opposes removing car parking spots. The two are unrelated. Reducing car parking spaces decreases accessibility and ease of access to the living environment for many residents.

207013.explanation: BBB wants to abolish the environmental zone in the inner city. The air quality is satisfactory. The zone only disadvantages residents and (market) entrepreneurs. The municipality must financially compensate entrepreneurs who are or have been disadvantaged by this policy.

207014.explanation: BBB values traffic safety and therefore opposes a generic implementation of 30 km/h speed limits. Not every street is the same. A generic reduction can worsen accessibility and does not lead to more safety.

207015.explanation: BBB wants more funding to combat human trafficking and organized crime. We believe there are sufficient cessation programs and oppose extra funding for these programs.

207016.explanation: BBB opposes making it mandatory but is sympathetic to the idea of food gardens. It is a great opportunity for young and old to get their hands in the soil and experience on a small scale what agriculture does for us every day.

206949.explanation: PVV Groningen does not support this. Citizen assemblies are not a good reflection of Groningen society. Decisions should be made by elected representatives, with clear participation for residents.

206950.explanation: PVV Groningen fully agrees with this. Groningers have the right to fast, clear, and fair damage compensation. No more hassle, just one desk and 100% damage restoration.

206951.explanation: PVV Groningen agrees with this. Groningers and entrepreneurs deserve tax cuts after years of increases. The municipality must better prioritize and stop costly ideological projects.

206952.explanation: PVV Groningen wants a student desk that practically helps students (including housing) while fairly maintaining pressure on the housing market. Clarity and quick assistance without bureaucracy.

206953.explanation: PVV Groningen believes the municipality should primarily focus on quality, price, and reliability. Additional sustainability and circular economy requirements make projects more expensive and increase costs for Groningers. Ideology must not dominate.

206954.explanation: PVV Groningen wants to make sharper choices: less money to large institutions and more support for heritage and local traditions. Culture should be recognizable to Groningers and remain affordable.

206955.explanation: PVV Groningen wants better youth care, less bureaucracy, and a thorough reorganization focused on quality. No cutbacks on children, but strict requirements and less profit motive. Stop discretionary working and improve legal position for families.

206956.explanation: PVV Groningen wants native homeless Groningers to be sheltered and quickly housed. The begging ban ensures nuisance can be addressed.

206957.explanation: PVV Groningen wants to allocate funds where truly needed: care, safety, and livability. Climate projects like WarmteStad increase living costs. That money should go to care plans for residents needing help.

206958.explanation: PVV Groningen opts for practical solutions: no ideological boycotts that make Groningen more expensive. Assess based on reliability, price, and benefit for the Groninger.

206959.explanation: Entrepreneurs already struggle due to high costs and regulations. Lowering the terrace tax helps hospitality businesses survive and ensures lively villages and neighborhoods without extra costs for residents.

206960.explanation: Advertising tax mainly impacts local SMEs. Abolishing it reduces burdens on entrepreneurs and keeps shops and hospitality in Groningen attractive and accessible. Entrepreneurs are not cash cows.

206961.explanation: PVV Groningen invests in livability in all neighborhoods and villages. Help where needed, without playing neighborhoods against each other. Groningen must be safe, tidy, and pleasant to live in everywhere. We fund investments by cutting climate projects: WarmteStad and RodeHaan.

206962.explanation: PVV Groningen believes entrepreneurs should decide their own opening hours. Fewer rules and more freedom to operate ensure lively neighborhoods and villages and help local entrepreneurs survive.

206963.explanation: PVV Groningen wants a complete asylum stop and closure of all reception centers in Groningen municipality. Thus, no shelter in residential neighborhoods either. The municipality's focus must be better housing, more safety, and better facilities for Groningers.

206964.explanation: PVV Groningen: Homes are for Groningers who have waited years. Priority for status holders is unfair, discriminates against Groningers, and increases the housing shortage. Equal rules for everyone. We must limit the influx of status holders and asylum seekers to reduce the housing shortage.

206965.explanation: PVV Groningen wants illegal residents to be detained and deported. Illegal stay in the Netherlands must be punishable. Groningen must stop providing bed, bath, and bread. Those millions can be better spent on Groningers and Groningen.

206966.explanation: PVV Groningen wants to limit the influx of labor migrants. First, help Groningers find work and housing. Labor migration increases pressure on housing, care, and livability in neighborhoods.

206967.explanation: The Stadjerspas is intended for Groningers with low incomes. PVV Groningen finds it inappropriate to extend this scheme to refugees while many Groningers struggle to make ends meet.

206968.explanation: PVV Groningen wants space for entrepreneurs and jobs. Extra sustainability requirements exclude businesses and harm the local economy. Establishment should focus on employment, accessibility, and legal compliance. PVV wants to support the Groningen entrepreneur.

206969.explanation: Angling is a calm, permitted recreational activity that suits Groningen. A ban is unnecessary and patronizing. The municipality should focus on real nuisance and crime.

206970.explanation: PVV Groningen is against new bans. Entrepreneurs should be able to make their own choices. Extra rules make doing business more expensive and drive hospitality venues out of the city and villages. Stop trying to "improve" the world and stop making life harder for everyone.

206971.explanation: Proper maintenance ensures neat, safe, and clear neighborhoods. PVV Groningen wants to stop excessive ecological management and opts for livability and traffic safety for residents. Properly maintaining villages and neighborhoods is valuing and respecting your residents.

206972.explanation: PVV Groningen is against forced gas-free policies. Residents must be able to choose for themselves. Obligations make living more expensive and burden people with costs, while alternatives are not always reliable. Moreover, everyone contributes via property tax (OZB) to cover WarmteStad's financial deficits.

206973.explanation: WarmteStad is a financial risk and enforces heating obligations. PVV Groningen wants to stop this experiment and opposes a municipal energy company that locks residents into expensive systems. Setbacks have been and will be covered through the budget, paid for by everyone via OZB or other levies.

206974.explanation: Agricultural land is meant for food production, not solar parks. PVV Groningen wants to protect the landscape and focuses on solar panels on roofs, not on open fields.

206975.explanation: PVV Groningen supports greenery, but not expensive forest projects that come at the expense of housing, agriculture, or accessibility. Green spaces must fit the environment and remain affordable.

206976.explanation: The livability and nighttime peace of residents come first. PVV Groningen wants to limit nuisance and enforce closing times, especially in residential neighborhoods and villages.

206977.explanation: Additional sustainability requirements make events more expensive and unfeasible. PVV Groningen wants room for traditions and events without ideological conditions.

206978.explanation: PVV Groningen considers safety essential. A safe space for victims of sexual violence or intimidation helps with swift support and reporting, without trivializing the problem.

206979.explanation: PVV Groningen does not want fewer festivals, but better distribution across Groningen and enforcement. Events belong to Groningen, as long as nuisance for residents is limited and closing times are respected.

206980.explanation: PVV Groningen opposes normalization of drug use. The focus should be on enforcement, prevention, and tackling dealers, not on facilitating drug use.

206981.explanation: PVV Groningen wants to keep sports accessible for children from families who cannot afford it. A general scheme is unnecessary and expensive. Targeted support is fairer and more effective.

206982.explanation: PVV Groningen is against identity politics. Street names should unite and fit Groningen’s history, not political reinterpretations of the past.

206983.explanation: The municipality should focus on the current problems of Groningen’s residents. PVV Groningen opposes organizing and funding commemorations that divide instead of unite.

206984.explanation: Carbide shooting and Easter fires are valuable Groningen traditions. PVV Groningen wants to preserve these, provided they are organized safely. Traditions connect villages and are part of our identity.

206986.explanation: Education should focus on basic skills such as language and arithmetic. Education about art and culture is not a core task of the municipality and must not come at the expense of education and care.

206987.explanation: PVV Groningen supports supporter involvement. The club belongs to the city and its residents, not to commercial investors who have no connection to Groningen.

206988.explanation: PVV Groningen is against a super-expensive pop venue. That money can be better spent on safety, care, and livability. Large cultural prestige projects are not a priority.

206989.explanation: BOAs increasingly face aggression. PVV Groningen believes they must be able to defend themselves to perform their work safely and effectively.

206990.explanation: The municipality must be neutral. Enforcement officers represent the government and should not wear religious or political symbols during their work. This causes resistance and undermines the authority of BOAs.

206991.explanation: More enforcement means more safety. PVV Groningen wants extra BOAs on the streets to visibly and directly tackle nuisance, crime, and insecurity.

206992.explanation: Camera surveillance helps prevent and solve crime. PVV Groningen uses this targetedly to increase the safety of visitors, entrepreneurs, and residents.

206993.explanation: PVV Groningen wants new-build homes to be available to people with local ties. This gives Groningen residents a fair chance at an affordable home again.

206994.explanation: PVV Groningen wants social rental housing to be first available to Groningen residents. Social rental is necessary, especially for starters and seniors with local ties. No priority for status holders.

206995.explanation: High-rise buildings can contribute to housing in the city, provided they are well integrated. PVV Groningen wants building where possible, without overburdening neighborhoods. Sufficient parking must be provided at these flats so that Groningen residents can work, live, and enjoy life.

206996.explanation: PVV Groningen wants housing for residents, not investors. Owner-occupation prevents speculation and helps starters and families find affordable homes. Student housing should be located on large campus sites.

206997.explanation: PVV Groningen values property rights. Extra taxes do not solve the housing shortage. The municipality should speed up construction and remove rules instead of introducing new levies.

206998.explanation: Aesthetic regulations delay construction and make homes more expensive. PVV Groningen wants faster building, fewer rules, and affordable homes for residents.

206999.explanation: PVV Groningen opposes additional pressure on the housing market. Student housing should be concentrated on campus locations, not in residential neighborhoods. Furthermore, PVV wants to restrict the influx of international students to reduce housing pressure for students.

207000.explanation: PVV Groningen permits building where necessary, but protects the Green Lung between Haren and Groningen. Housing must be careful with attention to livability and landscape.

207002.explanation: PVV Groningen opposes forced dispersal of social rental housing for ideological reasons. Neighborhoods must remain livable and not be additionally burdened. In new neighborhoods, social rental homes can be directly integrated.

207003.explanation: Those able to work must participate. Socially useful work provides structure, helps people move forward, and is fair to working Groningen residents.

207004.explanation: Basic jobs help people distant from the labor market to gain work and routine. PVV Groningen sees this as an alternative to long-term benefits.

207006.explanation: Anyone who refuses work without a valid reason should receive reduced benefits. Work must pay off, and welfare is a safety net, not a final destination.

207007.explanation: PVV Groningen wants to help children from Groningen families with little money with school costs, bicycles, and laptops. This gives every child equal opportunities, without bureaucracy.

207008.explanation: Fraud undermines support for social provisions. Reporting points help tackle abuse and keep the system fair for everyone.

207009.explanation: PVV Groningen opposes car harassing. Cars are absolutely necessary for many residents and families. Traffic measures must be practical and aimed at increasing safety, not ideological.

207010.explanation: Removing parking spaces makes neighborhoods and shopping areas inaccessible. PVV Groningen wants sufficient parking spots for residents and entrepreneurs. We do listen.

207011.explanation: Parking is not a luxury. Lower fees keep the city accessible and support SMEs and visitors.

207012.explanation: PVV Groningen wants space for all means of transport. Fewer car parking spaces harm accessibility and drive people out of the city.

207013.explanation: Environmental zones mainly affect people with smaller budgets, the market, fairs, and entrepreneurs. PVV Groningen wants to abolish these zones.

207014.explanation: PVV Groningen opposes generic speed reductions. Traffic safety requires tailored measures, not one uniform rule everywhere.

207015.explanation: Those who want to stop sex work should receive help. PVV Groningen supports voluntary guidance, without promoting or normalizing the profession.

207016.explanation: PVV Groningen is against mandatory projects. Initiatives are allowed, but not imposed and not with public funds if that comes at the expense of core tasks.

206949.explanation: Residents can be involved in decision-making in many different ways. We must examine for each topic which form of participation is appropriate.

206950.explanation: The municipality cannot take over all tasks from the national government. But the municipality can take the lead. They must be given the opportunity by the national government to do so.

206951.explanation: The property tax is a way to contribute extra to the municipality's income, which we can then spend on people who are less fortunate or, for example, on more measures for a sustainable future for our children.
