206966.explanation: Employers are responsible for decent housing of labor migrants, but this does not have to be on or near the workplace. Otherwise, labor migrants are immediately homeless after dismissal. Many labor migrants are in a vulnerable position; we address this with the property brigade.

206967.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate in the municipality of Groningen, even if they have little money. It is also important for status holders and refugees that they can immediately participate in sports, culture, and children's activities with their families.

206968.explanation: We want a sustainable Groningen. We do this together with the many companies that want to settle here. Therefore, only companies that work sustainably may settle in Groningen. We set requirements about CO2 emissions, reuse of raw materials, and sustainable deployment of personnel.

206969.explanation: We want to improve the welfare of fish through better education about fishing in an animal-friendly manner. At the same time, Groningen's anglers enjoy nature, relax, and chat with friends during fishing. We want to preserve this, but in the most animal-friendly way possible.

206970.explanation: To combat climate change, we must use less energy. Burning fossil fuels to heat terraces consumes a lot of energy. By setting rules as a municipality, we provide clarity for entrepreneurs to switch to alternatives.

206971.explanation: Mowing less is good for nature and insects. This way, we preserve nature, protect biodiversity, and create greener neighborhoods and villages.

206972.explanation: In Groningen, we know why you don't want to be dependent on Groningen (or Russian) gas. We must wean off gas, which will also eventually require compulsory measures. This will be done very carefully, and we want sufficient subsidies and help if people cannot afford this themselves.

206973.explanation: Because we ourselves control the municipal heating company in Groningen, rates are much lower than in the rest of the Netherlands. We can also give discounts to residents of social housing and decide which neighborhoods the heat network will expand to. Selling it would be a huge waste!

206974.explanation: We want the municipality itself to control the construction of solar parks. The municipality uses the profit to help people with little money to become more sustainable. Between the panels, there is space for greenery where insects and other animals feel at home. This strengthens biodiversity, especially that of farmland.

206975.explanation: We are creating 'The Groningen Forest’: at least 60 hectares of new forest on new locations or near existing forests. With more trees, we strengthen biodiversity, add more greenery to enjoy, and combat climate change.

206976.explanation: We are proud of the lively municipality of Groningen. Events after 11 p.m. belong here too. Groningen is a municipality with many different residents and desires. We focus on a balance between desires for liveliness and peace, small and large, and with attention to nature.

206977.explanation: Sustainable events are necessary for the sustainable Groningen we are working towards. In granting permits, we set sustainability requirements where possible. We also invest significantly in the racetrack as an event location, aiming to be the greenest festival venue in the Netherlands.

206978.explanation: Groningen has a fantastic nightlife, and everyone should feel safe there. Through a pilot, we want to investigate whether a safe space can contribute to the safety of especially women and queer people in nightlife. This way, we ensure a safe nightlife for everyone.

206979.explanation: We are proud of the many festivals in Groningen. We strive for a balance between small and large festivals, liveliness and peace, and with attention to nature. We also focus extra on small festivals that are free to enter so that festivals are accessible to everyone.

206980.explanation: Groningen has a wonderful nightlife. This remains so only if everyone feels safe and risks are reduced. By allowing party drugs to be tested, we make nightlife better and safer. Testing also opens the conversation about the risks of drug use.

206981.explanation: Sports should be possible for everyone, and money should not be a barrier to exercise. By not making it free for everyone, we financially enable free access for parents and children with little money. This also includes money for sportswear and sports shoes.

206982.explanation: Groningen played an important role as a trading city in the trade of enslaved people. We want more attention for this past and its ongoing effects today, among other things by naming new streets after unsung heroes from our shared history, like at Tulaplein.

206983.explanation: Together we commemorate the slavery past, but we also reflect on its ongoing impact in the present. We do this in collaboration with minority communities by creating two monuments, making Keti Koti a local holiday, and structurally safeguarding Bitterzoet heritage.

206984.explanation: Carbide shooting and Easter fires are important traditions with a rich history. They are part of village life and bring people together. Of course, it is important to always pay attention to the safety of people and animals.

206986.explanation: Art and culture create connection, encounters, and entertainment. It is important that all children in Groningen get to know them. We expand the culture hopper and the Youth Fund for Sports & Culture. Through cultural education, we ensure all children come into contact with art and culture from an early age.

206987.explanation: Currently, there are no commercial investors involved, and we want to keep it that way. FC Groningen belongs to FC Groningen. If there is a need for a new owner, we opt for the supporters rather than commercial investors. The FC Groningen identity and long-term policy come before quick profit.

206988.explanation: With a large pop venue near the station, there is a place where big bands want to perform. That is good for culture in Groningen. Together with the cultural sector, Groningers, and entrepreneurs, we will create a pop venue we can be as proud of as the Forum. We are also working on improving the Oosterpoort.

206989.explanation: Borough officers (BOAs) do important work keeping our municipality safe. Unfortunately, aggression against BOAs is increasing. A baton does not prevent this; it can even contribute to further escalation and does not make our BOAs safer. The national government should invest more in the police instead of in batons.

206990.explanation: Everyone should be themselves in Groningen. The municipality sets a good example by allowing this among its own employees. We trust the neutrality of our BOAs, regardless of how they express their faith. Neutrality is something you practice and achieve through good education and training.

206991.explanation: Safety is important and requires sufficient and good enforcement through proper cooperation between BOAs, police, and for example security personnel. We invest broadly in safety by also investing in prevention and care solutions. More BOAs alone is not the solution.

206992.explanation: We are proud of Groningen's nightlife. Everyone must be able to enjoy it safely. We invest in safety by focusing on equal opportunities, care, prevention, and repression. Temporary camera surveillance as a last resort alongside other measures is also part of this.

206993.explanation: People from inside and outside Groningen can be valuable to our municipality. Housing development can prioritize people with local ties, and this deserves particular attention in Groningen’s villages.

206994.explanation: We need nurses and police officers, but also ICT specialists and road workers, for example. Choosing between people based on their profession is therefore impossible; we must mainly build faster, more, and affordably so there is housing for everyone.

206995.explanation: In area developments like Stadshavens and Suikerzijde, more than six floors can be built. We stand for good architecture and a lively municipality where people can live pleasantly and affordably. Tall buildings deserve good design and must fit well with the surroundings.

206996.explanation: Homes are meant to live in, not to make money from.

206997.explanation: We choose to build more homes in Groningen. With the current housing crisis, we do not accept owners leaving their properties unnecessarily empty for long. By taxing them, we ensure more homes and buildings become available to live in.

206998.explanation: Aesthetic regulations are agreements that ensure houses look good and provide clarity. They create a pleasant living environment and do not delay housing construction.

206999.explanation: Groningen is the most beautiful student city in the Netherlands. Every year, thousands of students choose Groningen as their new home. We are proud of this and happy about it. That is why we choose to invest in good and fair housing for students.

207000.explanation: Groningen is a compact, lively city to live in with short travel distances and good amenities. In the coming decades, we will build many new neighborhoods, such as Suikerzijde and Meerstad, for more and sufficient housing. We will not expand further on farmland at the expense of the Groningen landscape.

207002.explanation: We choose neighborhoods with affordable owner-occupied and social rental homes. All neighborhoods and villages should have room for poor, rich, young, and old. That is fair and ensures a pleasant society for everyone.

207003.explanation: Everyone should be able to rely on a sufficient and predictable income through work or benefits. Economic security is a right. This means that social assistance policy should be based on trust and possibilities. Mandatory reciprocal obligations do not fit.

207004.explanation: We believe everyone in Groningen should be able to participate, diploma or not. Whether you easily find a job or not. Basic jobs are a good and proven effective way to help people who find it hard to get a job into work. Therefore, we choose more basic jobs.

207006.explanation: Everyone should be able to rely on a safe and predictable income through work or benefits. Economic security is a right. Therefore, social assistance policy must be based on trust and what is possible. Coercion does not help to include everyone.

207007.explanation: The costs of school, bicycles, and laptops are unaffordable for some families. We want children from less wealthy families to be able to fully participate. Therefore, we find it fair that the municipality pays for this.

207008.explanation: We opt for a human scale in all applications and regulations. A tip line does not fit with this. Social assistance policy must be based on trust. No unnecessary checks or fines for mistakes.

207009.explanation: Many streets are dominated by cars. We can counter this by making cars guests. They then have to adjust to playing children and pedestrians. Cars are welcome, but life on the street is more important.

207010.explanation: The space on the street belongs to everyone. Parking takes up a lot of space, leaving less room for children, bicycles, and greenery. Fewer parking spaces and more space for play, cycling, and trees create a pleasant municipality for all.

207011.explanation: In Groningen, we pay a fair price for car parking, which also covers bicycle parking. We will maintain this. We distinguish between the first private car, visitors, or other cars. Exceptions are made for caregivers and social organizations.

207012.explanation: There are many activities and little space in our municipality. Therefore, you must choose how to use the space. Car parking spaces occupy a lot of public space. We want fewer parking spots and more space for play, socializing, nature, and cycling.

207013.explanation: Car emissions cause air pollution. The environmental zone ensures fewer commercial vehicles drive in the city center. As a result, the air in the city is cleaner and healthier. Clean air is vital for people.

207014.explanation: Driving a maximum of 30 km/h is better for safety, health, and nature. Where possible, we want to make Groningen’s streets safer for playing children, cyclists, and pedestrians in this way. This also makes Groningen healthier.

207015.explanation: Sex work is work. In the municipality of Groningen, we help people who want another job, so we also help sex workers. When there is exploitation of sex workers, it is important the municipality helps them escape this through low-threshold programs with support.

207016.explanation: Food gardens nearby are very important. You meet your neighbors there and can grow healthy food, also for people without a large garden. Waiting lists show there is a shortage. We want to make space on, for example, municipal farmland so more food gardens can be created.

206949.explanation: A citizen’s assembly is a good way to make decisions that people recognize, because people themselves participate in what happens in their municipality. This can build more trust.

206950.explanation: People now get lost in a bureaucratic maze. This causes stress and uncertainty. We want one team with one contact point for damage, strengthening, and compensation. Personal, clear, and only finished when truly resolved.

206951.explanation: If possible, we will reduce property tax (OZB). But The Hague keeps cutting municipalities further, often removing that room. That is why we initially choose to maintain facilities and protect people with low incomes from municipal taxes.

206952.explanation: Students have the right to good support, but no separate department is needed for this. We want a municipality that helps everyone through clear, well-functioning counters. Less bureaucracy, more solutions-for students and other Groningers.

206953.explanation: By purchasing circularly and sustainably, the municipality saves money, items last longer, and we keep work and knowledge local. This gives community funds more value and supports companies that treat people and materials fairly.

206954.explanation: Heritage and local traditions deserve support, but not at the expense of other cultural forms. We choose a broad cultural policy in which neighborhood culture, amateur arts, and young creators also have a place.

206955.explanation: Too many children and parents wait too long for help. Budget cuts worsen and exacerbate problems. We choose to invest in youth care so help is close by, available faster, and families do not stand alone.

206956.explanation: A begging ban does not solve any problem. It pushes people with debts or without a roof over their heads further into difficulties. Poverty is not fought with fines, but with help, shelter, and prospects for a dignified life.

206957.explanation: You must not pit healthcare and climate against each other. Good climate policy lowers energy costs and is actually social policy against poverty. But if choices become unavoidable due to national cutbacks, we will always protect healthcare. People come before projects.

206958.explanation: Such an absolute ban is unworkable: the municipality would be unable to do business with almost anyone. This mainly affects small entrepreneurs who have no alternative yet. We do break ties with large fossil multinationals and invest in public, clean energy. We tackle the polluter, not the market trader.

206959.explanation: Terraces use public space to make money. It is therefore logical that a reasonable fee is charged in return. This way we can maintain public spaces properly and prevent costs from falling on residents who do not profit from them.

206960.explanation: We spare small entrepreneurs and social organizations. We specifically charge large advertising displays by big companies. They profit from our public space and should contribute fairly. This way, we keep the city pleasant and distribute the burdens fairly.

206961.explanation: Some neighborhoods have been underserved for years. It is therefore logical to invest extra there now. This ensures that everyone in Groningen can live decently-in every neighborhood.

206962.explanation: Unlimited opening hours favor large chains with many employees. Small shopkeepers are pressured to participate. With clear rules, we protect local shops that ensure vibrant neighborhoods and villages.

206963.explanation: Shelter must be fairly distributed across the country and within the municipality, including in wealthier areas. The national government must provide structural solutions. If truly necessary, we take responsibility ourselves.

206964.explanation: Everyone who urgently needs housing must be treated fairly. Refugees with a residence permit receive the same urgency as others who need housing quickly. Extra priority is unfair to other housing seekers.

206965.explanation: Stopping shelter for people without a valid residence permit helps no one. These are people who are not allowed to stay but cannot yet leave. Without shelter, they disappear from view on the streets, causing more unrest and nuisance.

206966.explanation: Living with your employer makes people dependent. This often leads to abuse. We demand decent housing and enforce strictly so that labor migrants live safely and neighborhoods are not harmed.

206967.explanation: The Stadjerspas is intended for residents with a low income, not for specific groups. Refugees can receive the pass once they live here and meet the income requirements. This keeps the scheme fair, simple, and equal for everyone.

206968.explanation: Sustainability is more than a green label for us. It also involves fair wages, permanent contracts, and good employer practices. New companies wanting to settle in Groningen must meet these standards. This is how we build a fair and clean economy.

206969.explanation: Angling is an affordable and relaxing form of recreation for many people. It is part of our city and villages. We see no reason to prohibit it.

206970.explanation: A ban is not necessary. Terrace heating must not waste energy; therefore, we set clear requirements on how heating is done. Sustainable solutions should be possible, so entrepreneurs can green their businesses and people can comfortably sit on terraces.

206971.explanation: Good maintenance is important. In areas that become untidy or unsafe, we mow more frequently. We do this smartly and ecologically: neat where needed, space for nature where possible. This keeps all neighborhoods cared for, safe, and pleasant to live in.

206972.explanation: No one is forced to just switch off gas. We believe in voluntary transition with good support. Gas-free construction for new buildings is already national policy. For existing homes: affordable, step by step, and in consultation with residents.

206973.explanation: By keeping Warmtestad in public hands, the municipality can manage affordable tariffs instead of profit. We want heating to become cheaper and fairer for Groningen residents, not more expensive.

206974.explanation: We do not exclude solar panels on agricultural land, but only if done carefully, publicly owned, and in consultation with local residents. The revenues must flow back to Groningen people, not to speculators.

206975.explanation: More forests and greenery provide cooling, cleaner air, and pleasant places to relax. We invest in new forest planting in places where Groningen residents truly benefit, with attention to nature and accessibility.

206976.explanation: Events make the city lively, but people must also be able to live and sleep. By setting clear boundaries on times and noise, we protect residents and keep Groningen livable, with room for enjoyment.

206977.explanation: Events must be organized sustainably, especially large festivals with significant impact. Small neighborhood activities have simple, feasible rules. This way, we make events more sustainable step by step without hindering volunteers and neighborhood initiatives.

206978.explanation: Going out must be safe for everyone. A safe space provides people a place to get immediate help in case of sexual violence or boundary-crossing behavior. This ensures quick support and makes nightlife areas safer.

206979.explanation: Festivals belong to the city but must not come at the expense of livability. We are not simply for fewer festivals but for less nuisance: clear limits on noise and sound days, and a fair spread across places and times.

206980.explanation: Drug testing prevents accidents and saves lives. Accessible places for safe testing must be available in nightlife areas. This is not encouragement but wise health policy that prevents harm.

206981.explanation: Every child must be able to participate in sports regardless of their parents' income. Sport benefits health, self-confidence, and fun together. Therefore, we ensure that fees for children are never a barrier to participation.

206982.explanation: It is good to make resistance heroes from the slavery past more visible in our city. But we do not want a fixed obligation. Naming should allow room for diverse stories from Groningen’s and shared history.

206983.explanation: The slavery past deserves a joint commemoration. By doing this together with social organizations, we ensure recognition, involvement, and a remembrance that unites and is supported by the city.

206984.explanation: Carbide shooting and Easter fires are local traditions that bring people together. We want to preserve them. However, we ensure clear agreements on safety, environment, and nuisance so everyone can enjoy them pleasantly.

206986.explanation: Every child must be able to experience art and culture regardless of home income. That stimulates creativity, self-confidence, and fun. By investing in this, we give children more opportunities to develop.

206987.explanation: We see with clubs like Vitesse how dangerous dependency on commercial investors can be. With supporters owning half, FC Groningen remains a club for Groningen people. A football club is not a toy for investors.

206988.explanation: We support a good pop venue near the station, but 'big' is not a goal in itself. It must fit needs and stay within budget. No prestige project but a sober, smart investment that does give Groningen a strong pop venue.

206989.explanation: Boeven (enforcement officers) are not police. Arming them increases the chance of escalation. We choose safety by investing in neighborhoods: more outreach workers, youth work, and good cooperation with community officers. This prevents problems instead of letting them get out of hand.

206990.explanation: A uniform is intended to be neutral and recognizable. Enforcement officers exercise authority on behalf of the government and must be clearly identifiable as such. Therefore, we follow the officers’ own line: one uniform, without religious expressions.

206991.explanation: There is too little enforcement in many neighborhoods and nightlife areas. More enforcement officers provide visibility and clarity, for example with illegal dumping, safer nightlife, and limiting drug-related nuisance. Enforcement must contribute to livable neighborhoods, not unnecessary repression.

206992.explanation: Camera surveillance can help in some situations but is not a solution in itself. Without enforcement and prevention, problems shift elsewhere. We deploy cameras targeted and temporarily only, as part of a broader safety approach.

206993.explanation: New construction must primarily benefit people who live, work, or study in Groningen. By fostering ties to the city, we ensure homes do not disappear to investors and neighborhoods remain livable for Groningen residents.

206994.explanation: Teachers, nurses, and police officers are essential to our city. If they cannot find affordable housing due to high living costs or part-time work, we must ensure they can live here. This keeps Groningen running.

206995.explanation: To tackle housing shortages, we must build, including upwards. But we do not do so haphazardly. Some locations are suitable for high-rise buildings, others are not. We choose per neighborhood what makes sense so new homes fit well into the area.

206996.explanation: Homes are for living in, not for making a profit. Investors and slumlords drive up prices and make housing unaffordable. With an owner-occupancy requirement, we ensure houses remain available for people who want to live here.

206997.explanation: Vacancy while people cannot find housing is unacceptable. With a vacancy tax, we intervene so properties are used again for living. Leaving them empty must not be a business model: the longer vacant, the heavier the consequences.

206998.explanation: Without aesthetic regulations, building quickly becomes a mess. With clear agreements, we ensure that new homes fit into the neighborhood and that Groningen remains beautiful and livable. We do not want Belgian-like situations in Groningen.

206999.explanation: Slumlords charge high rents and let buildings and neighborhoods deteriorate. This harms students and the living environment. Therefore, we support housing corporations in building affordable, good student housing so that students can live decently without causing nuisance to neighborhoods.

207000.explanation: There is a great housing shortage in villages. Sometimes building on agricultural land is necessary so people can continue living in their own village. We do this carefully, with respect for the landscape and the character of the village.

207002.explanation: Affordable rental housing must be possible in every neighborhood and village. Social housing should not be limited to a few neighborhoods. That’s why we build especially where it is currently lacking. This breaks the division and ensures Groningen remains one municipality.

207003.explanation: Those who do socially meaningful work deserve normal pay for it. Mandatory counter-performances are not a solution but disguised forced labor. We want to help people find real jobs with prospects, not unpaid work.

207004.explanation: Basic jobs give people for whom a regular job is not self-evident meaningful, paid work. They provide structure, self-esteem, and work that is important in neighborhoods and villages. That is why we expand the number of basic jobs to 1,000.

207006.explanation: Cutting social assistance benefits does not help anyone move forward. Sometimes working is temporarily not possible due to health, caregiving duties, or other problems. Then you need support and peace, not punishment. Cuts push people further into trouble.

207007.explanation: Every child must be able to participate at school, even if there is little money at home. That is why we reimburse bicycles, laptops, and other necessary school expenses. This prevents children from being sidelined by poverty.

207008.explanation: There are no special hotlines for other forms of fraud. Such a reporting point unfairly stigmatizes people receiving benefits and increases distrust. We choose equality and trust, with normal checks like for everyone else.

207009.explanation: In some streets, it makes sense for the car to be the guest, creating more space for pedestrians, cyclists, and playing children. This makes neighborhoods safer and more livable. We do this where appropriate, together with residents.

207010.explanation: Parking is already difficult in many neighborhoods. Simply removing spots increases pressure and shifts the problem. We maintain parking availability and look at each neighborhood's needs so people can park their cars nearby.

207011.explanation: Parking fees are too high for many residents and strain the monthly budget. Parking should not be a cash cow. We want lower and fairer rates so that people and their visitors can affordably park their cars in their own neighborhood.

207012.explanation: More bicycle parking spaces are welcome, but not at the expense of car parking spots. The city center must remain accessible to everyone, including those dependent on cars. We choose balance, not exclusion.

207013.explanation: Environmental zones must not exclude people or small entrepreneurs. The current zone rightly has many exceptions. We want tailored solutions and broad exemptions so that sustainability remains fair and affordable for everyone.

207014.explanation: 30 km/h makes streets safer and calmer. But this only works if roads are designed accordingly, with clear crossings and less cut-through traffic. We choose 30 km/h as the standard, except for ring roads and main roads.

207015.explanation: Those who want to stop sex work must receive good support. With guidance, care, and assistance toward other jobs, we increase safety and give people perspective. This is social policy without judgment.

207016.explanation: Food gardens can mean a lot for social interaction and sustainability. Where residents want this, the municipality must provide space and support. But not every neighborhood or village is suitable. We choose tailored solutions, not obligations.

206949.explanation: The democratic rule of law is the foundation of our society. As DURF, we would like to see permanent neighborhood councils that not only consult but also decide. Not only politicians should decide the municipality’s course; citizens must also have this right.

206950.explanation: Less paperwork, fewer rules, the citizen’s interest at the center – that is our commitment. If successful, we are in favor. That team must be given the decisiveness to tackle this; otherwise, it is just another counter.

206951.explanation: The price increases of houses and thus also the property tax are already high enough. Therefore, this tax on home ownership must be reduced.

206952.explanation: Students deserve attention; the municipal council represents them. But for DURF, this does not require yet another municipal department – DURF chooses freedom and lower costs. Also for students.

206953.explanation: Who pays for this? You do! So the municipality must purchase products and services smartly and cheaply. Local and sustainable? That’s fine but only if it costs nothing extra.

206954.explanation: DURF stands up for local interests. That is why DURF believes cultural subsidies should go to local traditions and heritage.

206955.explanation: The municipality should focus on simplification and cutting the large bureaucracy, not on the care itself. Care must always be available for citizens – and that can be done well without extra money.

206956.explanation: Livable and safe Groningen: no begging scene, but real help and shelter.

206957.explanation: Stop the wasteful climate policy that makes citizens poorer. Focus on real local needs like affordable care, or better yet: give the money back to Groningers through tax reductions.

206958.explanation: That makes no sense and is impossible. Almost half of the construction costs of the Forum were actually paid by NAM. Gasunie is one of the largest employers. UMCG is one of the city’s largest users of sour gas.

206959.explanation: DURF wants fewer taxes and more freedom. Therefore, we are against terrace taxes.

206960.explanation: DURF wants less tax and more freedom for citizens and entrepreneurs. Therefore, we oppose extra taxes on billboards, facade advertising, and illuminated advertisements.

206961.explanation: DURF wants to improve the livability of all neighborhoods, not just the poorer ones. But often these issues are most urgent in poorer neighborhoods.

206962.explanation: DURF supports freedom. Therefore, entrepreneurs should be able to determine their own opening hours.

206963.explanation: We do not give someone from another province priority over others. So why prioritize someone from another country? The municipality should focus on local housing problems. One effect of this policy is a pull factor.

206964.explanation: We believe asylum seekers are given too much priority over the interests of Groningers. If Groningers cannot find affordable housing and struggle to pay groceries, it cannot be explained that people from other countries are provided for.

206965.explanation: Interests of Groningers first – build affordable housing for locals. By the time we have solved this, there may be room for others.

206966.explanation: For DURF, it is not the municipality’s task to pay for housing for foreign workers. Local workers often cannot find affordable accommodation. Employers should solve this themselves.

206967.explanation: DURF supports equality. We are therefore against discrimination based on background. The Stadjerspas is for the poorest Groningers, regardless of whether they are refugees or not.

206968.explanation: DURF stands for freedom. Companies are welcome, providing jobs and services. Sustainability is a vague term. Companies must comply with legally established emission and pollution standards.

206969.explanation: DURF stands for freedom; practicing angling is part of this.

206970.explanation: DURF is against coercion and for freedom. If it is affordable for an entrepreneur to make the outdoors pleasant for guests without disturbing others, this is not a municipal matter. Moreover, this is simply a consequence of earlier policy: the indoor smoking ban.

206971.explanation: The municipality is responsible for proper maintenance of public spaces, also to improve traffic safety (visibility). As long as this remains, we have no preference for more or less frequent mowing.

206972.explanation: At DURF we are always against coercion, pressure, or other forms of influence on the freedom of choice. People who want to heat their homes with gas should be able to do so just as much as those who want to heat their homes with a heat pump.

206973.explanation: DURF stands for lower taxes and maximum freedom: companies that sustain themselves through voluntary contributions? At DURF, that always takes priority over subsidies from our wallets.

206974.explanation: It is absurd to sacrifice our beautiful fertile Groningen farmland for solar panels; food security is more important than a few meager kilowatts of electricity! Solar panels on roofs are fine, if the owner wants it. There is plenty of space available this way without having to use farmland.

206975.explanation: Let's first make room for our real problems. Space for housing, space for Groningen residents. DURF does not want to expropriate farmers. So how do you want to realize this? We prefer better maintenance and expansion of existing green spaces and parks.

206976.explanation: DURF supports festivals: they keep the city lively and interesting. The organizer should be able to continue longer, but in consultation with neighbors. Compensation for neighbors might be a solution.

206977.explanation: The municipality should facilitate business activity and events. This only leads to unnecessary regulatory burdens and costs for organizing a festival.

206978.explanation: The entire nightlife area must be safe. The owners of hospitality venues have this responsibility, and the police can assist them. Public safety is the police's task.

206979.explanation: If people want to organize or celebrate something, the municipality should facilitate that. A municipality should always encourage business activity and events because that keeps Groningen vibrant.

206980.explanation: No tax money for drug testing; entrepreneurs in the nightlife area can handle this perfectly well.

206981.explanation: Taxes must be lowered enough that people can always afford sports club memberships for their children.

206982.explanation: Proud of our culture and history: let's name new streets after icons from Groningen and the Netherlands.

206983.explanation: Let citizens commemorate or honor whoever they want themselves; this is not a matter for the municipality. We prefer to focus on combating modern slavery.

206984.explanation: Cultural expressions and traditions must be preserved. The municipality's role is to facilitate these, not to prohibit them.

206986.explanation: We already pay for art and culture. Art and culture should come from the people themselves. The municipality can simply require museums and cultural institutions to make art and culture accessible to youth in exchange for the existing subsidies.

206987.explanation: Groningen has a long-term interest in the club as cultural heritage. It is important that the municipality does not become financially involved. The municipality can help supporters with expertise and know-how to initiate this.

206988.explanation: Why should the taxpayer foot the bill for something with a limited target group? There are plenty of commercial parties who would want to build this, too. The only thing the municipality should do is make space for these kinds of initiatives.

206989.explanation: This is what the police are trained for. We cannot lower the bar for the monopoly on violence; that is simply too impactful.

206990.explanation: The government must always be neutral. We are against religious or cultural expressions by (law enforcement) officials.

206991.explanation: Doubling the number of BOAs would also mean doubling the costs. Additional taxes would need to be levied to finance this. DURF is not in favor. It is better to deploy BOAs more selectively to improve livability than to hire more.

206992.explanation: We support privacy and see camera surveillance as an infringement on personal privacy. It also undermines the idea that you are innocent until proven guilty. With camera surveillance, everyone is potentially suspicious. These systems can also be abused, including data leaks.

206993.explanation: How do you determine a connection? This sounds vague and arbitrary. Those who have been on the list longest have the most right to a home.

206994.explanation: This undermines the principle of equality. No one has more rights than others. It is also arbitrary; why should these people take precedence over others? Don't cleaners, mechanics, and garbage collectors have just as much right to housing?

206995.explanation: Densifying first: the smartest and fastest way to address the housing shortage; building within the existing city!

206996.explanation: Freedom over rules: banning buying houses for children or family? Or for renting out? What does this actually solve? Moreover, you would need to set up an organization to check people's homes. We consider that undesirable.

206997.explanation: Freedom first: such rules don't work; investors circumvent them with anti-squatting contracts, only the small private individual suffers.

206998.explanation: Yes, under conditions: relaxing building regulations to reduce red tape; building remains high-quality and fits our streetscape. Especially small renovations should be possible without approval.

206999.explanation: We want this for everyone because everyone has the right to housing. Housing is a basic need.

207000.explanation: Focus on densification within the built-up area. New construction should minimize impact on nature, but housing is an acute problem, so we agree.

207002.explanation: But this is already largely the case in Groningen city.

207003.explanation: Everyone has control over their own body and time. This is the definition of exploitation. We are firmly against coercion.

207004.explanation: The municipality may facilitate economic activity but does not have the mandate to create jobs and subsidize them with public money.

207006.explanation: DURF supports freedom: occupational choice must be free. The municipality should not interfere with career choices.

207007.explanation: We want tax reductions so that everyone can simply afford these kinds of things.

207008.explanation: Public funds must not be misused; that is why we are in favor.

207009.explanation: There are already more than enough restrictions on car traffic. Don't make driving in Groningen impossible.

207010.explanation: Groningen is a growing city; behind the fish market the parking garage was closed by the municipality. We are now actually lacking parking spaces!

207011.explanation: These rates must be substantially lowered. The current rates are unjustifiable.

207012.explanation: Groningen is a growing city; there must be more parking spaces for both cars and bicycles!

207013.explanation: Away with this policy: it only leads to more patronizing, higher costs for citizens and entrepreneurs, enforcement costs, fines, and administrative hassle.

207014.explanation: There are already more than enough restrictions on car traffic. Don't make driving in Groningen impossible.

207015.explanation: Why are we spending money on this? Are there no bigger problems?

207016.explanation: We are in favor, as long as the initiative comes from the citizens themselves. The municipality's role is only to facilitate when there is demand for it. Bureaucracy and costs for the community must be limited to an absolute minimum.

206949.explanation: Instead of making plans for residents, we make plans with residents. The citizen council, also in smaller forms (such as neighborhood or village councils), takes a prominent place here. It is important that underrepresented groups in society have a voice in this.

206950.explanation: Gas extraction has caused a disaster in Groningen. Many residents are still waiting for safe homes, settled damages, and genuine recognition. The government must take every step that contributes to damage repair. Help from the municipality lowers the threshold.

206951.explanation: It is fair that homeowners pay property tax (ozb). The strongest shoulders carry the heaviest burdens. The ozb tax is an important source of income for the municipality, which pays for amenities such as culture, greening, fire department, education, and maintenance of roads and parks.

206952.explanation: Students are exploited by landlords, and among other things due to study pressure, they more often face mental health issues. A student desk can help young people when they have questions about rental contracts, their rights, and where to turn with mental complaints.

206953.explanation: As a municipality, we must do everything possible to combat the climate and nature crises. We want Groningen to become the greenest municipality in the Netherlands, and for that, circular, sustainable, and local procurement is crucial. Plant-based catering, recycled textiles, and second-hand products become the norm.

206954.explanation: Culture may challenge, make people think, and must be diverse. Subsidies should therefore also go to small creators and not just to heritage. We believe animals should not be used for entertainment or traditions. The budget for art, culture, and heritage is significantly increased.

206955.explanation: Every young person has the right to good care. Promoting equal opportunities for everyone is necessary to prevent an increase in youth care. We tackle the overload of youth care adequately. We reduce waiting lists and solve bottlenecks in mental health care.

206956.explanation: It is a human right to ask others for help when you cannot support yourself due to circumstances. We should not criminalize vulnerable people but provide care as a municipality. We want to act only in cases of criminal exploitation of beggars.

206957.explanation: We want to invest in climate AND care. Over the past four years, we have shown that this is possible. By, for example, maintaining the property tax (OZB), we can invest in both.

206958.explanation: Everything points to us having already passed the 1.5-degree warming threshold. We can turn the tide if we make different choices NOW to combat the climate and nature crises. We break ties with fossil fuel companies and work towards a ban on fossil fuel advertising.

206959.explanation: This tax is already relatively low and is used for a pleasant and green living environment. That is a big advantage for entrepreneurs and residents.

206960.explanation: Advertising encourages overconsumption. Groningen must stop advertising in public spaces except for government campaigns, culture, and charities. Until then, there remains a tax on advertising.

206961.explanation: Good living conditions are a basic right. Poorer neighborhoods have much catching up to do compared to richer neighborhoods in many respects. Richer neighborhoods are often greener with more facilities. By investing heavily in poorer neighborhoods we ensure more equal opportunities.

206962.explanation: Entrepreneurs should have the freedom to open and close whenever they want. We will counter overconsumption, for example by limiting advertising.

206963.explanation: Refugees are welcome! We provide good reception, do not cooperate in the deportation of children, and maintain the bed-bath-bread scheme. We do not cooperate with immigration policies that violate human rights. We pay attention to vulnerable groups, such as LGBTQIA+.

206964.explanation: Everyone in a vulnerable position has priority for a rental home, including refugees. If the law against priority for status holders is adopted, Groningen will create a local priority scheme. A safe roof over your head is a basic right regardless of where you were born.

206965.explanation: No one is illegal! Everyone has the right to humane shelter regardless of residency status or papers. We maintain the bed-bath-bread scheme.

206966.explanation: Companies earn millions in the Netherlands by exploiting labor migrants. If they lose their job, they often lose their home too. By separating housing and employment, they are better protected. The municipality must protect vulnerable groups against unscrupulous landlords and employers.

206967.explanation: Everyone has the right to a dignified existence regardless of origin or residency status. With a Stadjerspas, they gain access to sports, culture, and veterinary costs, for example. This helps not only to prevent poverty but also ensures they can participate in Groningen from day one.

206968.explanation: Groningen must become the greenest municipality in the Netherlands. We make critical choices about which companies and sectors may establish here. No polluters, such as livestock farms or harmful industry. Sustainable startups and initiatives are encouraged and supported by the municipality.

206969.explanation: Fish are living beings that feel pain. Angling is therefore animal abuse. We work hard for a ban on angling. Until such a ban is established, we advocate for a ban on barbed hooks, lead fishing weights, and live nets. Animal welfare always comes before entertainment.

206970.explanation: Groningen, together with local entrepreneurs, tackles energy waste such as light pollution, terrace heating, and open shop doors. Sustainable alternatives are promoted.

206971.explanation: Flowers, insects, and animal habitats must be given full space. Less mowing helps address the nature crisis. Our mowing policy is phased and never takes place during breeding, mating, nursing periods, or the winter rest of animals.

206972.explanation: There is no time to lose in combating global warming. However, the energy transition must be fair. Residents who have to switch to a heating network are compensated if their contract proves more expensive.

206973.explanation: We want to keep Warmtestad 100% in our own hands. This allows us to manage what is done with the proceeds from those connections and ensure the income flows back to our residents. This way, we invest in the heating network, sustainable heat sources, and local neighborhood networks.

206974.explanation: Agricultural land will primarily be used for plant-based food and nature. If livestock farming shrinks considerably, there is also room for housing development. Solar panels preferably go on roofs, and only in exceptional cases do we choose solar meadows. There will be no solar parks in nature.

206975.explanation: To combat the nature crisis and preserve habitat for wild animals, forests are needed. Additionally, trees help against heat stress and strengthen biodiversity. The more trees, the better. The MEER forest is a great project by residents that we want to realize.

206976.explanation: No disruptive activities, such as events, are planned in or near nature reserves. For each type of event outside nature areas, the effects on local residents must be considered. A liveable Groningen for people and animals is central in our considerations.

206977.explanation: Events should be sustainable according to the Party for the Animals: free from waste, fireworks, polluting diesel generators, and gas-fired heating columns. We do not want events with animals. Subsidies are only granted to events that meet sustainability conditions.

206978.explanation: Everyone must be able to go out safely. Sexual violence and transgressive behavior are unacceptable. With 'safe spaces', we can create a safe place where people can turn to during unsafe situations. We want extra attention for the safety of women, racism, and people from the LGBTQIA+ community.

206979.explanation: Festivals are a valuable form of culture, but we take into account their impact on local residents, animals, nature, and the environment. Nature reserves and parks such as Zuiderplantsoen and Sterrebos are not suitable locations. More or fewer festivals depend on the impact on the surroundings.

206980.explanation: We expand low-threshold drug testing to ensure safety. At parties where hard drugs are likely used, people are present to provide information and possible care.

206981.explanation: We want all children to be able to play sports and grow up healthily. Free membership can make choosing to play sports much easier, especially for children from poorer families. We do believe parents with higher incomes can contribute to the children’s sports club.

206982.explanation: Groningen’s slavery and colonial past has left deep marks, the effects of which are still noticeable today. Street names in Groningen should do justice to the history of all residents. Diversity will be the starting point for this.

206983.explanation: Groningen supports and facilitates important commemorations and holidays of various residents, such as Eid, Yom Kippur, and Keti Koti. Groningen’s slavery and colonial past have left deep marks, the effects of which are still noticeable today.

206984.explanation: Easter fires cause enormous air pollution that settles in nature. Furthermore, birds often build nests in the piles, making it a danger to animals. Loud bangs like fireworks and carbide cause a lot of fear among both pets and wild animals.

206986.explanation: Art and culture are indispensable in democracy. They prompt reflection, release emotions, move and unsettle. Children must be able to develop socially, emotionally, motorically, and creatively. Culture contributes to this.

206987.explanation: FC Groningen belongs to Groningen and is very important to many Groningers. We support this process but cannot contribute financially very much. In weighing, the costs and benefits and the contributions and vision of residents, supporters, and FC Groningen will be decisive.

206988.explanation: We have the best cultural offering in Northern Netherlands, and by investing in the renovation of De Oosterpoort and building De Grote Popzaal, we ensure future generations will also have high-quality culture in the city.

206989.explanation: Violence is not the solution. Moreover, institutional racism and ethnic profiling are problems within the police and enforcement. People from Groningen with a migration background may face disproportionate violence more often when enforcement officers are armed.

206990.explanation: Groningen follows the advice of the Human Rights Council and the European Court. Enforcement officers are allowed to wear religious expressions. This gives more people the opportunity to fulfill the role of enforcement officer.

206991.explanation: We want a government that protects citizens. The municipality ensures there are enough enforcement officers who collectively represent a representative reflection of society. Doubling their number is not necessary. Additional efforts, for example through green enforcement officers, are.

206992.explanation: Privacy concerns lead to very cautious use of camera surveillance in public spaces. Camera surveillance offers only a false sense of security and often only causes the problem to be displaced. Automatic facial recognition is too intrusive and will not be used.

206993.explanation: Housing must be fairly distributed. Everyone must have the same opportunities. In villages, it can help to give young people the possibility to continue living in the village. Housing corporations in the city already have a system of appropriate allocation.

206994.explanation: There is a shortage of social rental housing. Everyone who has the right to social rental housing must have equal opportunities. That is what the Party for the Animals stands for. We do support granting priority for vulnerable positions.

206995.explanation: High-rise buildings are one of the solutions to build houses, but also to apply greening. High-rise buildings should be concentrated near amenities such as supermarkets and larger public transport facilities. Livability must not be affected.

206996.explanation: Housing is a right, not a profit model. We preserve the owner-occupancy requirement and combat vacancy. We believe the municipality should immediately apply purchase protection where possible, in order to exclude investors from neighborhoods.

206997.explanation: As far as the Party for the Animals is concerned, measures will be taken against speculation with housing and vacancy, such as an owner-occupancy requirement and a vacancy ordinance with sanctions. A tax can also be effective. Long-term vacant properties will be made habitable.

206998.explanation: We want to build homes for the coming decades, not just for the next few years. Aesthetic regulations help prevent poor quality housing or homes that do not fit well in the environment. Homes should be sustainable, healthy, and beautiful.

206999.explanation: We want students to afford rooms, starters to find rental or owner-occupied housing that fits their situation, and seniors to move to accessible senior housing. A suitable home for everyone.

207000.explanation: Agricultural land is primarily intended for healthy food production and nature development. If livestock farming shrinks, there is space for housing. However, we mainly focus on infill development in the city and villages. For example, building nature-inclusive at the edges of neighborhoods.

207002.explanation: Social rental homes must be distributed over all neighborhoods and villages. We aim for a municipality where people meet each other regardless of income. This must be nature-inclusive, and the greenery remains preserved.

207003.explanation: Groningen must shift its focus from profit to welfare. Social assistance is an important safety net, and enforcing counter-performance actually increases inequality and stress. The municipality should primarily help people.

207004.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes no one deserves stress about income. Through basic jobs, people can do meaningful work. The interests of a person on benefits are central here.

207006.explanation: The Participation Act, with its strong emphasis on paid work and its coercive nature, has failed. Groningen places the resident and their needs at the center and has a broader and more generous view of those receiving social assistance. The municipality also recognizes unpaid work as participation.

207007.explanation: Equal opportunity starts with children. Therefore, extra attention will be given to children growing up in poverty or with a language disadvantage. The municipality must ensure every child can participate, regardless of their parents’ income. We can contribute to this through special assistance and allowances.

207008.explanation: According to the Party for the Animals, there will be no whistleblower hotline about welfare fraud in Groningen. The existing hotline will be stopped. Trust in our residents must be central within the government. We do not want situations like the childcare benefits scandal.

207009.explanation: We discourage polluting traffic and encourage clean traffic. We give plenty of space to pedestrians and cyclists. This contributes to climate goals, recovers space for greenery on our streets, and makes it safer outside for children and animals.

207010.explanation: As far as we’re concerned, no new parking spaces are added on the street. For new construction, the lowest possible parking standard is used. The municipality removes parking spaces in desired locations and converts them into wider sidewalks, children’s play areas, flower-rich verges, small parks, or bicycle parking facilities.

207011.explanation: Polluting cars pay more, clean ones less. Residents can park their cars at a lower rate in nearby parking garages or cheaply at park-and-ride facilities, where they can use free bicycles and affordable public transport. Permits and street parking become more expensive.

207012.explanation: The municipality must remove parking spaces at desired locations and convert them into wider sidewalks, children’s play areas, flower-rich verges, small parks, or bicycle parking facilities. There will also be enough bicycle parking at major public transport stops and transfer points.

207013.explanation: Clean air is extremely important for people, animals, and nature. Polluting traffic is therefore banned by expanding the environmental zone to the entire built-up area. A zero-emission zone remains in effect for commercial traffic in the city center.

207014.explanation: For motorized traffic within the built-up area, a maximum speed of 30 km/h should apply. Road design will be adapted where possible, which also creates space for greening and playing in the street.

207015.explanation: Policy is made with sex workers, not about them. Their voices lead. Groningen guarantees safety for sex workers, with respect for their self-determination. Offering help is our priority. We will continue the supervised house.

207016.explanation: In every village, neighborhood, and district there should be space for a food garden and a community center where people meet and can eat together. This brings people closer, reduces health disparities, and increases equal opportunity.

statements.title: The municipality should reduce the number of parking spaces.
The municipality should not do business with companies that use fossil fuels.
The municipality should urge the national government to create a single municipal team where people can handle everything related to earthquake damage.
Most of the cultural subsidies should go to heritage and local traditions.

statements.title: All refugees in the municipality should receive a Stadjerspas. That is a pass for people with low incomes.
The municipality should allow events to continue later in the evening.

titleParts.glossary: A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.

statements.title: The municipality should spend the money for climate policy on healthcare plans.
In entertainment districts there should be multiple places where people can have drugs tested for safety.
The municipality should help supporters of FC Groningen become half owners of the football club instead of commercial investors.
The municipality should abolish the advertising tax. That is a tax on facade ads, signs, illuminated advertisements and the like.
The municipality should pay the membership fees of a sports club for all children under 16.

statements.theme: Priority for teachers, nurses, and police
statements.title: Teachers, nurses and police officers should be given priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: Leraren, verpleegkundigen en politieagenten moeten voorrang krijgen bij een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Employers must ensure that migrant workers live at or near their workplace.

titleParts.glossary: People from another country who work temporarily in the Netherlands.

statements.title: Groningen should distribute social housing across the whole municipality.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should set up a student affairs department that also employs students.
Groningen should sell the municipal heating company WarmteStad.
Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) should be allowed to carry a baton.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.
Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

statements.title: The maximum speed should be 30 km/h everywhere, except on the ring road and a few main roads.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.

statements.title: In new construction, half of the rental homes should go to people who have ties to Groningen.
The municipality should have at least 60 hectares of new forest planted.
The municipality should abolish the aesthetic rules for building and renovating homes.
The municipality should have a large concert venue built on the south side of the station.
The option to report benefit fraud on the municipality's website should remain.
Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
The municipality should lower the tax on terraces.
The municipality should stop providing shelter to people who do not have a valid residence permit.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met de opvang van mensen die geen geldige

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.

statements.title: There should be more streets where the car is "a guest."
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more money to introduce children to art and culture.
Every village and neighborhood should get its own community food garden.

shootoutStatements.title: In the city center of Groningen there should be more bicycle parking spaces and fewer car parking spaces.
The municipality should set up a citizens' assembly on important topics to advise the municipal council. A citizens' assembly consists of residents selected by lot.
The municipality should no longer provide emergency shelter to asylum seekers. Emergency shelter consists of temporary accommodation sites.
The municipality should mainly procure circular and sustainable products and services.
In new construction in urban areas, apartment buildings of more than six storeys may be allowed.
The municipality should allow carbide shooting in Ten Post and Easter bonfires in Onnen and Noordlaren.
The municipality should invest more in improving livability in poorer neighborhoods. This may come at the expense of richer neighborhoods.
The municipality should spend more money on student housing.
People on social assistance who refuse an offered job should receive reduced benefits.
The municipality should ban recreational angling.
The municipality should allow new construction on agricultural land.
The number of municipal enforcement officers (boa's) should be doubled.
The municipality should allow fewer festivals.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.
The municipality must not make cuts to youth care.
The ban on begging should remain in place.

shootoutStatements.theme: Priority for people with refugee status
shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social rental housing.
Shopkeepers and hospitality businesses should be able to set their own opening hours.
Only companies that operate sustainably should be allowed to establish themselves in the municipality of Groningen.
The municipality should mow roadside verges and public grass more often.
The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
The municipality may not force anyone to stop using natural gas.
The municipality should only permit events that are organized sustainably.
No fields of solar panels should be allowed on agricultural land.
Nightlife areas should have a safe space where people can go in the event of sexual violence or harassment.
There should be more CCTV surveillance in Groningen's city center.
The municipality should name new streets and squares after resistance heroes from the history of slavery.
Parking rates in the municipality of Groningen should be lowered.
The municipality should organize the annual commemoration of the history of slavery together with civil-society organizations.
The low-emission zones in Groningen should be removed.
Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) should be allowed to wear a headscarf, yarmulke, or turban.
The municipality should spend more money on programs for sex workers who want to stop.
Owners who leave their properties empty for longer than six months should pay a tax to the municipality for that.
The municipality should provide more basic jobs; basic jobs are subsidized positions for people who face barriers to the labor market.
The municipality should cover the cost of bicycles, laptops and school expenses for children from low-income families.
